- performance issues found: preview resolves the same workbook URLs as import, reads existing DrugMaster price/expiry rows in 200-record chunks, caps sampled rows at 100, and reuses the same change-event comparison helper as import. No new dependency, render path, unbounded DB read, or unbounded response payload was added.

Medical-safety review also found three import-integrity blockers: non-empty malformed YJ values could be accepted as canonical master identities, an existing GTIN could be silently reassigned to another DrugMaster, and package-unit detection could reuse a package-code column as `package_quantity_unit`.
- security risks found: reduced broad package-master mutation risk by adding `dryRun: true` support that parses and normalizes HOT source rows without creating import logs or writing `DrugMaster` / `DrugPackage`. HOT preview/import now reject malformed YJ values before master/package writes, treat existing-GTIN cross-master conflicts as manual-review skips instead of reassigning package identity, and prevent GTIN/JAN columns from being stored as package units. The preview uses the existing HOT URL policy, returns no PHI, and does not expose credentials. No auth/RLS rule, schema migration, live DB mutation outside tests, external send, push, deploy, secret handling, or destructive operation was performed.
- performance issues found: HOT preview parses the official source once, performs one bounded `DrugPackage.findMany` for valid GTIN candidates, caps sampled rows at 100, and reports upsert-oriented counts that match the real import's write/conflict units. Normal import also prefetches candidate package GTINs once to avoid per-row conflict reads before upserts. No new dependency, render path, broad unbounded DB read, or unbounded response payload was added.
- validation commands: `pnpm exec prettier --write` on the four HOT service/route/test files and ledgers/docs; focused `pnpm exec vitest run src/server/services/drug-master-import/hot.test.ts src/app/api/drug-master-imports/hot/route.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=60000`; scoped ESLint, scoped Prettier check, and scoped `git diff --check` on the HOT files; `pnpm typecheck`; `pnpm typecheck:no-unused`; `pnpm lint`; `pnpm format:check`; full `git diff --check`; API-contract and medical-safety subagent reviews/re-review.

Package-code normalization was also duplicated across barcode verification, QR OTC promotion, and the DrugPackage backfill analyzer, increasing the chance that a 13-digit JAN, 14-digit GTIN, and legacy fallback would be treated differently in different workflows.
- security risks found: reduced medication data-integrity and patient-safety risk by creating package-level rows only when HOT input supplies a valid JAN/GTIN/package-code column and a YJ-linked DrugMaster exists. HOT code itself is not treated as a GTIN. Invalid package codes are counted and skipped. No live DB migration was applied, and no auth, RLS, external send, push, deploy, secret handling, or destructive operation changed.
- performance issues found: HOT import adds one `DrugPackage.upsert` only for imported YJ-linked rows with a valid package code. Shared normalization avoids divergent lookup expansion. No new source fetch, dependency, broad scan, unbounded loop, render path, or N+1 read path was added.

- performance issues found: no DB query shape, dependency, retry loop, external request, synchronous blocking, or unbounded work was added. The patch only wraps existing responses, narrows serialized payloads, and adds focused assertions.

- bugs found: generated proposal creation could supersede open proposals or create idempotency batches before transaction-time billing rejection; generated/drawer proposals did not reject active schedule collisions; generated proposals did not reject same case/date/pharmacist open-proposal collisions outside the supersede scope or duplicate generated route cells; manual and generated schedule creation only guarded duplicate schedules by case/date/type plus `cycle_id`, allowing a second active visit on the same case/date/type with a different cycle; manual/generated schedule creation did not reject open proposal collisions for the same case/date/type; proposal batch billing validation did not count accepted drafts cumulatively; proposal idempotency P2002 recovery attempted to read inside the aborted transaction; special-cap proposal generation needed stale transaction-time cap and fingerprint coverage; serializable retry paths lacked coverage for retry-time duplicate conflicts.
- security risks found: reduced patient-safety and billing-integrity risk by moving proposal billing validation and active schedule checks inside the serializable transaction before side effects, rejecting duplicate open/active schedule conflicts, and keeping `@db.Date` schedule tests green across JST/UTC/negative-offset runtimes. Auth/RLS, permission checks, schema, live DB data, external send, push, deploy, secret handling, and destructive DB operations were not changed.
- performance issues found: no new unbounded loops, external calls, dependencies, or synchronous blocking were added. Additional duplicate and billing checks are scoped to single case/date/pharmacist scheduling writes and run inside existing transactional write paths.
- validation commands: `pnpm exec prettier --write src/app/api/visit-schedule-proposals/route.ts src/app/api/visit-schedule-proposals/route.test.ts src/app/api/visit-schedules/generate/route.ts src/app/api/visit-schedules/generate/route.test.ts src/app/api/visit-schedules/route.test.ts src/lib/validations/visit-schedule.ts src/server/services/visit-schedule-service.ts`; `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/visit-schedule-proposals/route.test.ts src/app/api/visit-schedules/route.test.ts src/app/api/visit-schedules/generate/route.test.ts 'src/app/api/visit-schedules/[id]/route.test.ts' --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; scoped ESLint on the seven schedule files; scoped `git diff --check` on the seven schedule files; `pnpm typecheck`; `pnpm typecheck:no-unused`; `pnpm lint`; `pnpm format:check`; `pnpm date-slices:check`; `TZ=Asia/Tokyo pnpm test:schedule-time:tz`; `TZ=UTC pnpm test:schedule-time:tz`; `TZ=America/Los_Angeles pnpm test:schedule-time:tz`.

### 20260630-0500 JST

- bugs found: patient board "today" semantics used runtime-local `localDateKey(now)` both for the `visit_schedules.scheduled_date >= today` query lower bound and for `visit_today` card/chip classification. Under UTC or negative-offset runtimes during Japan early morning, the board could query from the previous `@db.Date` sentinel and fail to classify the Japan-business-day visit as today.
- security risks found: reduced PHI-bearing patient-board misrouting risk by aligning the omitted "today" board, `visit_today` chip, and today visit counts to Japan civil days. Auth/RLS, no-store responses, response minimization, patient href encoding, contact/address omission, and UTC midnight `@db.Date` display via `formatUtcDateKey` were preserved. No schema migration, live DB mutation, external send, push, deploy, or secret handling changed.
- performance issues found: no DB query shape, dependency, external request, retry loop, synchronous blocking, or unbounded work was added. The schedule-time TZ gate now includes one additional focused route test file.
- validation commands: `TZ=UTC pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/patients/board/route.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; same focused command under `TZ=Asia/Tokyo` and `TZ=America/Los_Angeles`; `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/patients/board/route.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; scoped ESLint on the route/test files; scoped `git diff --check`; scoped Prettier check on package plus route/test files; `pnpm date-slices:check`; `TZ=UTC pnpm test:schedule-time:tz`; `TZ=Asia/Tokyo pnpm test:schedule-time:tz`; `TZ=America/Los_Angeles pnpm test:schedule-time:tz`; attempted `pnpm format:check`.

- bugs found: weekly `INTERVAL>1` RRULEs were phased per weekday instead of per ISO week, windowed regeneration could restart the phase from the requested window start, monthly intervals were similarly anchored to the current generation window, invalid BYDAY tokens were silently dropped, and delayed visit-record completion could re-phase the next suggested biweekly visit from the delayed record date instead of the original scheduled date.
- security risks found: reduced medication/visit scheduling data-integrity and patient-safety risk by making recurring visit dates fail closed on invalid BYDAY input and by keeping delayed visit-record suggestions on the original schedule series. No auth, authorization, RLS policy, schema, migration, live DB mutation, PHI logging, external send, push, deploy, or destructive operation changed. Japan domestic business dates remain `Asia/Tokyo`; UTC is limited to Prisma `@db.Date` date-only sentinel encoding and runtime-TZ regression tests.
- performance issues found: no new dependency, external request, broad DB scan, N+1 query, or unbounded server loop was added. The RRULE parser still iterates only the bounded requested date window and pre-DB validation now rejects invalid BYDAY tokens before care-case and shift loading.

- bugs found: the day-board API returned only the capped first `3` pending proposals, while the UI treated `pending_proposals.length` as the total pending count; this could falsely show a near-empty unresolved queue and hide additional patient-contact/reschedule work. Reviewer follow-up also found the first count implementation could read all hidden IDs even when no hidden rows existed and lacked personal-dashboard hidden-task scope coverage.
- security risks found: reduced PHI exposure risk by returning only aggregate counts for hidden proposals/tasks and by not expanding hidden patient names, free text, task titles, task descriptions, or metadata. No auth, authorization, RLS policy, schema, migration, live DB mutation, external send, push, deploy, or destructive operation changed. Japan domestic business dates remain `Asia/Tokyo`; UTC is limited to Prisma `Date` sentinel encoding and runtime-TZ regression tests. `date` omitted day-board requests now use `japanDateKey()` instead of process-local date keys so Japan-only operation is not silently reinterpreted as UTC.
- performance issues found: avoided expanding hidden proposal/task details; hidden proposal ID and hidden task count queries now run only when `hidden_count > 0`. Existing visible proposal cap remains unchanged.

The route now rejects client-supplied `drug_code` / source/status identity fields, rejects mixed content edits, preserves source-code evidence, rejects deterministic conflicts, keeps org/assignment scope and OCC, and writes structured patient-linked audit entries without expanding PHI. No auth permission, RLS policy, schema, migration, live DB, deploy, push, or destructive operation was changed.
- performance issues found: no new dependency, external request, unbounded loop, or broad scan was added. The identity branch adds at most one DrugMaster lookup by selected id and one bounded resolver lookup over the existing line's `source_drug_code` and `drug_code`; normal non-identity line edits keep their existing query shape except for additive selected identity fields.

### 20260629-1244 JST

- current task: support Claude's FE lane first, then finish `F-20260629-005` pharmacy stock usage-mismatch code resolution for receipt/HOT/YJ QR usage codes after Claude approval.
- files inspected: agmsg inbox/history/send for `phos/codex`, `git status --short --untracked-files=all`, `docs/ui-ux-design-guidelines.md`, `.agent-loop/FEATURE_QUEUE.md`, `CODEX_GOAL_PROGRESS.md`, `src/app/api/pharmacy-drug-stocks/usage-mismatch/route.ts`, `src/app/api/pharmacy-drug-stocks/usage-mismatch/route.test.ts`, `src/app/(dashboard)/patients/[id]/medications/medications-content.tsx`, `src/app/(dashboard)/patients/[id]/medications/medications-content.test.tsx`, and local Next.js route handler / `unstable_rethrow` docs under `node_modules/next/dist/docs/`.
- files changed: `src/app/api/pharmacy-drug-stocks/usage-mismatch/route.ts`, `src/app/api/pharmacy-drug-stocks/usage-mismatch/route.test.ts`, `.agent-loop/FEATURE_QUEUE.md`, `CODEX_GOAL_PROGRESS.md`, and this Ralph state entry. Existing unrelated dirty files, Claude FE locked `medications-content.tsx` files, Prisma schema/migrations, DB data, deploys, commits, pushes, and destructive operations were preserved.
- bugs found: `GET /api/pharmacy-drug-stocks/usage-mismatch` was treating QR draft `drugCode` as a 12-character YJ-like value by applying `.slice(0, 12)` and then matching only `DrugMaster.yj_code`. That could leave 9-digit receipt codes unmatched and truncate 13-digit HOT codes before lookup, causing coded QR usage to appear as unrecognized or unstocked even though `DrugMaster.receipt_code` / `hot_code` had the canonical mapping. Claude approved the fix and noted a non-blocking theoretical DB row-order dependency when the same source code appears in multiple DrugMaster code families; Codex removed that dependency by building the lookup map in explicit YJ, receipt, HOT priority order. During Claude FE review, separate false-empty bugs were found in `medications-content.tsx`, including the main `medication-profiles` path and second-order medication-issues consumers.
- security risks found: no auth, RLS, PHI projection, DB schema, or permission surface was weakened. The usage-mismatch route continues to avoid name-based DrugMaster matching, so display-name-only QR rows remain unresolved. The implementation adds internal receipt/HOT lookup fields but projects `matched_drug` back to the previous safe display shape, avoiding a response payload expansion.

### 20260629-1129 JST

- current task: harden `POST /api/drug-master-imports/manual-clinical` and fix its `DrugAlertRule` global-baseline replacement semantics.
- files inspected: agmsg inbox/send for `phos/codex`, `git status --short --untracked-files=all`, `src/app/api/drug-master-imports/manual-clinical/route.ts`, `src/app/api/drug-master-imports/manual-clinical/route.test.ts`, `src/server/services/drug-master-import/manual.ts`, `src/server/services/drug-master-import/manual.test.ts`, `src/server/services/drug-master-import/shared.ts`, earlier `DrugAlertRule` hybrid RLS review context, `CODEX_GOAL_PROGRESS.md`, and this Ralph state file.
- files changed: `src/app/api/drug-master-imports/manual-clinical/route.ts`, `src/app/api/drug-master-imports/manual-clinical/route.test.ts`, `src/server/services/drug-master-import/manual.ts`, `src/server/services/drug-master-import/manual.test.ts`, `CODEX_GOAL_PROGRESS.md`, and this Ralph state entry. Existing unrelated dirty files, Claude-owned FE files, Prisma schema/migrations, DB data, deploys, commits, pushes, and destructive operations were preserved.
- bugs found: manual clinical import still used generic `withAuthContext` plus a manual `isAdmin` check, ran import writes without explicit RLS context, and lacked route-local no-store/fixed-error metadata-only logging. The service's `replaceAlertRules` also deleted `DrugAlertRule` rows by `alert_type` only, which is unsafe now that `DrugAlertRule` is hybrid (`org_id: null` global baseline plus org-specific rows); an import of global baseline PIM/high-risk rules could delete org-specific tenant tuning. Claude review classified the `org_id: null` replacement-scope change as a critical data-safety fix because the previous delete could wipe org-specific tenant alert-rule tuning.
- security risks found: reduced manual clinical import cache/raw-log/RLS drift risk by converting POST to route-local `requireAuthContext({ permission: 'canAdmin' })`, `runWithRequestAuthContext`, `withOrgContext(ctx.orgId, ..., { requestContext: ctx, maxWaitMs: 10_000, timeoutMs: 30_000 })`, `withRoutePerformance`, `withSensitiveNoStore`, `unstable_rethrow`, fixed `internalError()`, and metadata-only `logger.error`. The service now replaces only global baseline alert rules with `where: { alert_type, org_id: null }` and explicitly creates replacement rules with `org_id: null`, preserving org-specific alert rules. The manual clinical import also carries the earlier behavior change that requires `yj_code` for clinical rule write identity and rejects name-only fuzzy `drug_name contains` matching before DrugMaster lookup, reducing the risk of applying PIM/high-risk/renal/safety metadata to the wrong same-name/different-code drug.
- performance issues found: preserved the existing manual clinical import service flow and route response shape. No new network call, dependency, N+1 path, or unbounded loop was added; the import now runs in one RLS-scoped transaction because it touches hybrid `DrugAlertRule` and global DrugMaster/package insert/import log tables.
- validation commands: `pnpm exec prettier --write 'src/app/api/drug-master-imports/manual-clinical/route.ts' 'src/app/api/drug-master-imports/manual-clinical/route.test.ts' 'src/server/services/drug-master-import/manual.ts' 'src/server/services/drug-master-import/manual.test.ts'`; `pnpm exec vitest run 'src/app/api/drug-master-imports/manual-clinical/route.test.ts' 'src/server/services/drug-master-import/manual.test.ts' --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `pnpm exec eslint --max-warnings=0 'src/app/api/drug-master-imports/manual-clinical/route.ts' 'src/app/api/drug-master-imports/manual-clinical/route.test.ts' 'src/server/services/drug-master-import/manual.ts' 'src/server/services/drug-master-import/manual.test.ts'`; `pnpm exec prettier --check ...`; `git diff --check -- ...`; `pnpm typecheck`; `pnpm typecheck:no-unused`.
- validation results: manual clinical route and service tests passed `2` files / `12` tests. Scoped ESLint, Prettier, diff-check, `pnpm typecheck`, and `pnpm typecheck:no-unused` passed.
- remaining work: none for this manual-clinical slice. Claude approved the RLS/no-store/fixed-error and `DrugAlertRule` global-baseline scope fix, independently reran the route/service tests (`12/12` passed), and requested ledger clarification only.
- next action: continue the next DrugMaster import route hardening slice after any newer Claude request is handled.

### 20260629-0414 JST

- current task: prioritize Claude inbox, then harden `GET`/`PATCH`/`DELETE /api/set-batches/[id]` while preserving manual set-batch edit/delete semantics and auditability.
- files inspected: agmsg inbox for `phos/codex`, `git status --short --untracked-files=all`, Next route-handler and `unstable_rethrow` docs, `src/app/api/set-batches/[id]/route.ts`, `src/app/api/set-batches/[id]/route.test.ts`, `src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts`, `src/app/api/__tests__/protected-patch-delete-routes.test.ts`, `src/lib/dispensing/set-batch-history.ts`, `src/server/services/prescription-access.ts`, `src/lib/auth/visit-schedule-access.ts`, `prisma/schema/prescription.prisma`, the set-batch history migration FK, and auth/request-context/RLS/sensitive-response/logger/performance helpers.
- files changed: `src/app/api/set-batches/[id]/route.ts`, `src/app/api/set-batches/[id]/route.test.ts`, `src/app/api/__tests__/protected-patch-delete-routes.test.ts`, `CODEX_GOAL_PROGRESS.md`, and this Ralph state entry. `src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts` was inspected and validated but not newly changed in this slice. Existing unrelated dirty files, DB state, migrations, deploys, and destructive operations were not touched.
- bugs found: set-batches detail GET/PATCH/DELETE still used generic `withAuthContext`. GET and DELETE pre-read used direct `prisma.setBatch.findFirst`, so request-context metadata for RLS/audit triggers was not explicitly propagated. PATCH/DELETE mutation `withOrgContext` calls lacked explicit `{ requestContext: ctx }`. PATCH/DELETE were absent from the protected PATCH/DELETE auth matrix. Medical-safety review also found a High auditability bug: DELETE inserted `manual_delete` change log after deleting the parent batch while still writing the relational `batch_id`, which can violate the `SetBatchChangeLog.batch_id` FK in real PostgreSQL.
- security risks found: reduced set-batch PHI cache, raw-error leakage, and RLS/audit metadata risk by using route-local `requireAuthContext`, `runWithRequestAuthContext`, `withSensitiveNoStore`, `withRoutePerformance`, `unstable_rethrow`, fixed `internalError()` fallback, metadata-only `logger.error`, and explicit `{ requestContext: ctx }` for detail reads and PATCH/DELETE transactions. Preserved `canSet`, id/org scoping, assignment scoping, line detail projection, PATCH JSON/schema validation, version OCC, setting-only guard, updateMany/deleteMany claims, `manual_update`/`manual_delete` logs, DELETE version requirement, notifyWorkflowMutation payloads, and no body parsing before auth. DELETE audit logs now omit relational `batch_id` while retaining the deleted batch id inside `before_snapshot`, avoiding the FK failure while keeping audit evidence.
- performance issues found: preserved route observability with `withRoutePerformance`; no DB query shape, dependency, N+1 path, or unbounded loop was added.
- validation commands: `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run 'src/app/api/set-batches/[id]/route.test.ts' --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts -t "set-batches/\\[id\\] GET" --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/app/api/__tests__/protected-patch-delete-routes.test.ts -t "set-batches/\\[id\\]" --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; scoped ESLint for set-batches detail/protected files; scoped Prettier check for the same files; focused `git diff --check` for the same files; `pnpm typecheck`; `pnpm typecheck:no-unused`; read-only code-mapper and medical-safety Codex reviewers.
- validation results: set-batches detail route Vitest passed `1` file / `15` tests after the DELETE audit-log FK fix. Protected GET target passed `3` tests / `363` skipped. Protected PATCH/DELETE target passed `6` tests / `56` skipped. Scoped ESLint, Prettier check, focused diff whitespace check, `pnpm typecheck`, and `pnpm typecheck:no-unused` passed. Medical-safety reviewer initially found the High DELETE audit-log FK issue, then confirmed it resolved after omitting relational `batch_id` from delete logs and keeping the deleted id in `before_snapshot`.
- remaining work: send Claude a `PATCH_REVIEW_REQUEST` for set-batches detail and address any findings. Continue remaining backend PHI/RLS/no-store routes after Claude inbox drain.
- next action: drain agmsg; if no Claude blocker is pending, continue the next high-value backend route after review request.

- files changed: `src/lib/pharmacy/qr-intake-mapper.ts`, `src/lib/pharmacy/__tests__/qr-intake-mapper.test.ts`, `src/app/api/qr-scan-drafts/route.ts`, `src/app/api/qr-scan-drafts/route.test.ts`, `src/app/api/qr-scan-drafts/[id]/confirm/route.ts`, `src/app/api/qr-scan-drafts/[id]/confirm/route.test.ts`, `src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.ts`, `src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts`, `docs/drug-code-master-architecture.md`, `CODEX_GOAL_PROGRESS.md`, and this Ralph state entry. Claude FE commits were reviewed read-only; existing unrelated dirty files, Prisma schema/migrations, DB state, external sends, deploys, and destructive operations were not touched.
- bugs found: QR/JAHIS DrugMaster lookup mixed code candidates and name candidates. If a QR line had no usable drug code or had a bad code but a name-only DrugMaster match, the mapper treated that name fallback as a resolved DrugMaster, copied `yj_code` into `line.drug_code`, suppressed `unmatchedDrugs`, and allowed QR draft confirmation/import to persist the guessed YJ while `skipStructuringCheck` was enabled.
- security risks found: reduced medication data-integrity and patient-safety risk by treating DrugMaster name matches as review-required candidates only, preserving null/original source codes instead of replacing them with candidate YJ codes, persisting resolution status/source/candidate metadata into QR draft parsed data, and blocking QR draft confirm/import before claim/create when any line remains `review_required` or `unresolved`.
- performance issues found: no new DB query, dependency, N+1 path, or unbounded loop. The added checks are bounded over QR draft lines already in parsed data.
- validation commands: `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/lib/pharmacy/__tests__/qr-intake-mapper.test.ts src/app/api/qr-scan-drafts/route.test.ts 'src/app/api/qr-scan-drafts/[id]/confirm/route.test.ts' src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; scoped ESLint for the eight QR/intake files; scoped Prettier check for the same files plus the design doc; focused `git diff --check`; `pnpm typecheck`; read-only API contract and medical-safety Codex subagent reviews. Claude Wave1 cluster1/cluster2 reviews also ran scoped ESLint/Prettier/diff checks on their FE commit files.
- validation results: focused QR/intake Vitest passed `4` files / `152` tests. Scoped ESLint, Prettier check, focused diff whitespace check, and `pnpm typecheck` passed after tightening the mapper type guards and explicit return type. API contract and medical-safety reviewers both identified the High name-fallback-to-YJ risk before implementation; the final patch follows their recommendation. Claude Wave1 cluster1 `f9f9a957` and cluster2 `35b861e8` were both approved after read-only review and validation.
- remaining work: send Claude a `PATCH_REVIEW_REQUEST` for this QR/JAHIS slice and address any findings. The next product slice should add the pharmacist UI/API path to resolve `review_required` candidates into a confirmed `drug_code`/future `drug_master_id`.
- next action: send Claude the QR/JAHIS review request, then drain inbox before choosing the next backend task.

### 20260629-0005 JST

- current task: prioritize Claude review messages, then learn the official drug-code layers and remove a same-name medication comparison ambiguity from the dispensing workbench BFF.
- files inspected: agmsg inbox for `phos/codex`, `git status --short --untracked-files=all`, official drug-code sources from MHLW/SSK/PMDA/GS1/MEDIS, `prisma/schema/drug.prisma`, `prisma/schema/prescription.prisma`, `src/lib/pharmacy/qr-intake-mapper.ts`, `src/lib/dispensing/dispense-barcode-verification.ts`, `src/lib/prescription/medication-diff.ts`, `src/lib/prescription/medication-diff.test.ts`, `src/app/api/dispense-tasks/[id]/workbench/route.ts`, `src/app/api/dispense-tasks/[id]/workbench/route.test.ts`, and medical-safety subagent findings.
- files changed: `src/app/api/dispense-tasks/[id]/workbench/route.ts`, `src/app/api/dispense-tasks/[id]/workbench/route.test.ts`, `docs/drug-code-master-architecture.md`, `CODEX_GOAL_PROGRESS.md`, and this Ralph state entry. Existing Claude/user dirty files, Prisma schema/migrations, DB state, external sends, deploys, and destructive operations were not touched.
- bugs found: `matchMedicationDiffLines` already matched prescription lines code-first, but the workbench BFF converted its result back into a `drug_name` keyed `changeQueuesByName`. Same-name/different-code rows could therefore display a `change_type` on the wrong row. Medical-safety review also found that `comparison` rows did not expose drug codes, leaving UI/API consumers unable to distinguish same-name rows even after backend matching was fixed.
- security risks found: reduced medication data-integrity and patient-safety risk by removing name-keyed comparison queues, deriving `change_type` directly from the matched current/previous line pair, and exposing `current_drug_code` / `previous_drug_code` in comparison rows. No auth, permission, PHI logging, RLS, schema, migration, or write-path behavior changed.
- performance issues found: removed an intermediate name-keyed queue and did not add DB queries, dependencies, N+1 paths, or unbounded loops.
- validation commands: `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run 'src/app/api/dispense-tasks/[id]/workbench/route.test.ts' src/lib/prescription/medication-diff.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; scoped ESLint for the workbench route/test; scoped Prettier check for the workbench route/test and drug-code design doc; focused `git diff --check`; `pnpm typecheck`; medical-safety Codex subagent review.
- validation results: focused Vitest passed `2` files / `23` tests. Scoped ESLint, Prettier check, focused diff whitespace check, and `pnpm typecheck` passed. Medical-safety reviewer found one Medium response-shape gap and one Low-Medium nullable-code policy gap; the Medium gap was fixed and retested. The nullable-code invariant is documented as the next schema/intake migration slice because legacy/import flows still allow `PrescriptionLine.drug_code = null`.
- remaining work: design and implement the expand/contract migration for `PrescriptionLine.drug_master_id`, `source_drug_code`, `source_drug_code_type`, and `drug_resolution_status`; make QR/JAHIS name fallback explicitly unresolved/review-required; add package-unit DrugPackage/GTIN modeling for GS1/JAN scans.

- performance issues found: improved observability by adding `withRoutePerformance(req, ...)`; added `runWithRequestAuthContext(ctx, ...)` as request-context defense-in-depth while preserving explicit org filters and existing count query semantics. Added one bounded `medicationCycle.count` aggregate for prescription-registration linkage monitoring, with no per-row fetch or N+1 query.

- files inspected: `git status --short --untracked-files=all`, agmsg inbox/send for `phos/codex`, `node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/01-getting-started/15-route-handlers.md`, `node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/03-api-reference/04-functions/unstable_rethrow.md`, `src/app/api/dashboard/monthly-stats/route.ts`, `src/app/api/dashboard/monthly-stats/route.test.ts`, `src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts`, adjacent dashboard routes, `src/lib/auth/context.ts`, `src/lib/auth/request-context.ts`, `src/lib/utils/performance.ts`, `src/lib/utils/logger.ts`, and focused diffs/status after implementation.

- bugs found: the dashboard monthly-stats GET returned patient monthly-stat data with explicit no-store behavior, but still depended on the common `withAuthContext` wrapper for auth/error handling. That wrapper can log raw handler errors on ordinary unexpected failures, and the route lacked route-local PHI-safe structured logging, route performance metrics, and explicit request auth-context propagation.
- security risks found: reduced cache/log/body leakage risk by wrapping success, auth failure, validation, and handled 500 responses with `withSensitiveNoStore`; converting ordinary unexpected failures to fixed `internalError()`; preserving Next.js control-flow errors with `unstable_rethrow(err)`; logging only route/method/status and allowlisted error names; and testing that raw patient text, insurance-related text, SQL text, stack text, crafted error names, and the raw error object are absent from response/log surfaces. Existing `canViewDashboard` permission, month parsing/defaults, explicit `org_id` scoping, visit outcome filter, patient lookup select, insurance-basis/monthly-limit logic, status ordering, success `{ month, summary, patient_stats }` shape, DB schema/data, migrations, external sends, push/deploy, and destructive-operation boundaries were not changed.
- performance issues found: improved observability by adding `withRoutePerformance(req, ...)`; added `runWithRequestAuthContext(ctx, ...)` as request-context defense-in-depth while preserving explicit org filters and both existing Prisma query shapes.
- validation commands: `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/dashboard/monthly-stats/route.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts -t "dashboard/monthly-stats GET" --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `pnpm exec eslint src/app/api/dashboard/monthly-stats/route.ts src/app/api/dashboard/monthly-stats/route.test.ts`; `pnpm exec prettier --check src/app/api/dashboard/monthly-stats/route.ts src/app/api/dashboard/monthly-stats/route.test.ts`; `git diff --check -- src/app/api/dashboard/monthly-stats/route.ts src/app/api/dashboard/monthly-stats/route.test.ts`; privacy compliance reviewer; medical safety reviewer; general Codex reviewer; verifier subagent; Claude checker review over agmsg.
- validation results: focused route Vitest passed `1` file / `9` tests. Protected-route filtered Vitest passed `3` tests with `363` skipped. Scoped ESLint, scoped Prettier check, and focused diff whitespace check passed. Privacy, medical safety, and general reviewers reported no findings. Verifier subagent re-ran the focused gates and reported all passed. Claude checker independently ran Vitest `9/9` plus scoped ESLint, verified exactly the same two Prisma queries before/after, and sent `APPROVED`.

- performance issues found: no normal-path application performance change. Added targeted indexes for `PharmacyOperatingHours(org_id)`, `(org_id, site_id)`, `(site_id, weekday)`, and the two `BusinessHoliday` partial unique indexes needed for deterministic duplicate prevention.
- validation commands: duplicate preflight via Node/pg for org-wide and site-specific `BusinessHoliday` groups; `pnpm exec prisma format`; `pnpm exec prisma validate`; `pnpm exec prisma db execute --file prisma/migrations/20260627090000_add_pharmacy_operating_hours/migration.sql`; local DB catalog verification via Node/pg for columns, constraints, indexes, partial indexes, RLS, policy, and trigger; `pnpm db:generate`; `pnpm exec vitest run tools/scripts/verify-migration-preconditions.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; manual BusinessHoliday duplicate read-only query after precondition-script update; scoped ESLint for `prisma/seed.ts` and precondition files; scoped Prettier check for `prisma/seed.ts` and precondition files; scoped diff whitespace check for all S2 files; `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false --incremental false --project tsconfig.json`; `pnpm typecheck:no-unused`; db_steward subagent read-only review.
- validation results: duplicate preflight returned org-wide `0` and site-specific `0`, so no delete/cleanup was needed. Prisma format/validate passed. Local S2 migration apply passed after two existing local DB drift repairs: missing `PharmacySite_id_org_id_key` was made a guarded no-op/repair in the S2 migration, and missing existing RLS/audit support functions plus nullable AuditLog context columns were repaired locally only from prior migration definitions. DB catalog verification confirmed `BusinessHoliday.open_time/close_time`, `PharmacyOperatingHours` columns/checks/FKs/indexes, two `BusinessHoliday` partial unique indexes, RLS/FORCE, tenant policy, and audit trigger. Prisma Client generation passed. Precondition unit tests passed `1` file / `12` tests and manual BusinessHoliday duplicate read-only query returned `0/0`. Scoped ESLint/Prettier/diff checks passed. Full TypeScript passed. `typecheck:no-unused` passed. `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false db:verify-migration-preconditions` now loads `.env` but cannot complete on this local dev DB because the DB is missing older `PatientInsurance.public_program_code`; that is recorded as an existing local drift blocker, not an S2 diff failure.
- remaining work: none for S2. Implementation/precondition files landed as `f2353f7c`, progress state as `7d0fa744`, Claude approved after Codex confirmed local-dev-only migration apply and read-only catalog verification. Prod/RDS remains separately gated.

- bugs found: `withAuthContext` used the raw string `logger.error` overload for unexpected handler failures, which serializes `Error.message` and `stack`; thrown DB/client errors can contain patient names, insurance numbers, SQL detail, or other PHI even though the client response is fixed.

Tests prove PHI-like thrown text is omitted from the fixed 500 response, logger context, console payload, and production Sentry message payload, and that `Sentry.captureException` is not used for the safe structured path.

- bugs found: workbench read fetches did not pass the active org header, so real-data reads could fall back to the wrong org and show false empty/error states. `/set` also treated a successful no-SetPlan calendar queue as an acquisition failure and rendered `実データ未取得`/`実データを取得できませんでした` even when the API read itself succeeded with no calendar-backed work.
- security risks found: no auth, authorization, permission, RLS policy, schema, migration, DB write, external send, secret, audit log, or mutation behavior changed. Org scoping was tightened for client read fetches by sending the current `x-org-id`.
- performance issues found: no new polling, write path, DB read fan-out, or backend workload was introduced. The client reuses the active org scope and distinguishes empty vs failed calendar reads without extra normal-path network calls.
- validation commands: `pnpm vitest run src/components/features/dispense-workbench/dispensing-workbench.adapter.test.ts src/components/features/dispense-workbench/use-workbench-mutations.test.tsx src/components/features/dispense-workbench/use-workbench-view.test.ts src/app/api/dispense-workbench/patients/route.test.ts src/lib/dispensing/dispense-workbench-shared.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `pnpm eslint src/components/features/dispense-workbench/dispensing-workbench.adapter.ts src/components/features/dispense-workbench/dispensing-workbench.tsx src/components/features/dispense-workbench/use-workbench-mutations.ts src/components/features/dispense-workbench/use-workbench-view.ts src/components/features/dispense-workbench/dispensing-workbench.adapter.test.ts src/components/features/dispense-workbench/use-workbench-view.test.ts`; `pnpm exec prettier --check src/components/features/dispense-workbench/dispensing-workbench.adapter.ts src/components/features/dispense-workbench/dispensing-workbench.tsx src/components/features/dispense-workbench/use-workbench-mutations.ts src/components/features/dispense-workbench/use-workbench-view.ts src/components/features/dispense-workbench/dispensing-workbench.adapter.test.ts src/components/features/dispense-workbench/use-workbench-view.test.ts`; `git diff --check -- src/components/features/dispense-workbench/dispensing-workbench.adapter.ts src/components/features/dispense-workbench/dispensing-workbench.tsx src/components/features/dispense-workbench/use-workbench-mutations.ts src/components/features/dispense-workbench/use-workbench-view.ts src/components/features/dispense-workbench/dispensing-workbench.adapter.test.ts src/components/features/dispense-workbench/use-workbench-view.test.ts`; read-only local Prisma/API checks; authenticated Playwright desktop/mobile checks on `http://localhost:3012/dispense` and `/set`.

- bugs found: visit-record detail GET returned SOAP/receipt/reason/attachment/baseline-context PHI without sensitive no-store headers; unexpected read failures could escape as framework errors instead of a fixed no-store API envelope; protected GET matrix did not assert 401/403 no-store for the detail read; catalog/meta tests did not explicitly pin the base visit-record list/detail entries; GET reads happened outside `withOrgContext` even though `VisitRecord` is RLS/FORCE RLS protected; and the full record spread could return `visit_geo_log` while only deleting `patient_state_snapshot`.
- security risks found: reduced visit-record PHI/cache, tenant-isolation, geolocation, snapshot, and raw-error exposure risk by wrapping handled GET responses and unexpected failures with `Cache-Control: private, no-store, max-age=0` and `Pragma: no-cache`, returning a fixed `INTERNAL_ERROR` 500 envelope without raw error text, running all GET reads through `withOrgContext(ctx.orgId, ..., { requestContext: ctx })` and scoped `tx` delegates, and removing both `patient_state_snapshot` and `visit_geo_log` before the raw success response spread. Existing `canVisit`, `org_id`, assignment access, baseline context derivation, attachment normalization, raw top-level success shape except the two sensitive fields, PATCH behavior, schema, migrations, external sends, and destructive operations are unchanged.
- performance issues found: none materially changed. Valid detail reads keep the same logical query set but now execute under the RLS context; no new DB reads or N+1 paths were introduced.

- bugs found: dispensing-stats GET returned operational dashboard metrics without sensitive no-store headers and was missing from curated route catalog/protected GET matrix coverage despite being a statistics page allowlisted fetch. It also returned `completedLast7Days`, an exact 7-day daily activity trend that the current statistics UI validated but did not display.
- security risks found: reduced operational metric cache and overexposure risk by wrapping exported GET responses so handled 200/401/403 responses carry `Cache-Control: private, no-store, max-age=0` and `Pragma: no-cache`; registering `/api/dashboard/dispensing-stats` as `canViewDashboard`; and narrowing the API/caller schema to the three displayed raw KPI fields (`pendingTasks`, `auditPendingTasks`, `completedToday`). Tenant-boundary tests now assert all three count queries include `org_id: ctx.orgId` plus the intended status/audit/today filters. Existing `canViewDashboard`, org scoping, raw non-enveloped success contract, 403 locked-state caller behavior, malformed payload fail-closed UI, schema, migrations, external sends, and destructive operations are unchanged.

### 20260626-0012 JST

- current task: continue Codex-only backend/API/privacy hardening by strict-validating explicit query values and no-store wrapping all `GET /api/prescription-intakes` responses.
- files inspected: agmsg inbox; `git status --short --untracked-files=all`; local Next.js Route Handlers guide from the prior route-hardening loop; `src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.ts`; `src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts`; `src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts`; prescription/search callers in `src/app/(dashboard)/prescriptions/prescriptions-workspace.tsx`, `src/app/(dashboard)/search/search-content.tsx`, and `src/lib/search/categories.ts`; `src/lib/api/sensitive-response.ts`; `src/lib/auth/context.ts`; gbrain call-graph output from the pre-edit inspection; API-contract and privacy subagent reports.

- bugs found: `GET /api/prescription-intakes` silently normalized malformed explicit list/search filters: blank `q`, `status`, `source_type`, `care_tags`, and `include_total` were treated as omitted/false; padded values were trimmed or passed into enum checks inconsistently; overlong `q` was truncated to 100 characters; duplicate query keys used first-value semantics. This could widen a user-intended scoped prescription intake query or make malformed search input hit DB. The route also returned prescription/patient list/search data without sensitive no-store headers, and handler-only no-store wrapping would have missed auth/permission failures returned by `withAuthContext`.
- security risks found: reduced PHI/PII cache and overfetch risk by wrapping the exported GET route so 200, validation 400, auth 401, and permission 403 responses receive `Cache-Control: private, no-store, max-age=0` and `Pragma: no-cache`; malformed explicit query values now fail before assignment-scope and prescription-intake DB reads; validation errors use fixed field messages without echoing query values. Existing `canVisit` permission, assignment access scoping, valid omitted-filter list behavior, valid single-value status/source/care-tag filters, valid `include_total=1`, valid `include_total=0`, search minimal projection, POST create flow, schema, migrations, external send, and destructive operations are unchanged.
- performance issues found: malformed query requests now return before assignment/search where construction and before `prescriptionIntake.findMany`/`count`. Valid and omitted-filter requests keep the existing `limit + 1` cursor-page query behavior and optional count path.
- validation commands: focused `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/__tests__/protected-post-routes.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; caller compatibility `pnpm exec vitest run 'src/app/(dashboard)/prescriptions/prescriptions-workspace.test.tsx' 'src/app/(dashboard)/search/search-content.test.tsx' --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `pnpm exec eslint src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.ts src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts`; `pnpm exec prettier --write src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.ts src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts`; `pnpm exec prettier --check src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.ts src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts`; `git diff --check -- src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.ts src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts`; long-gate `pnpm typecheck` under token `E8717502-4C9D-4FC9-9665-75BA7C880E3F`.

### 20260625-2200 JST

- current task: follow up on the patient-share-cases privacy review by minimizing `patient_share_cases_viewed` audit payloads for list views.
- files inspected: agmsg inbox; `git status --short --untracked-files=all`; `src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.ts`; `src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.test.ts`; `src/lib/audit-log.ts`; `src/app/api/audit-logs/export/route.ts`; the prior patient-share-cases privacy reviewer finding; local validation output for the patient-share-cases and protected-route suites.
- files changed: `src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.ts`, `src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.test.ts`, and this Ralph state entry.
- bugs found: `GET /api/patient-share-cases` wrote page-level share-case, base-patient, base-site, and partner-pharmacy id arrays into `patient_share_cases_viewed` audit `changes`, and used the first returned share case as the aggregate list-view `targetId`. Unfiltered list views therefore persisted row-level identifiers unrelated to a single target and exposed those identifiers through audit-log consumers.
- security risks found: reduced privacy/audit-minimization risk by replacing row id arrays with count-only fields and using the stable aggregate `targetId: patient_share_cases` for list-view audits. Patient-specific filtered views still set `patientId` from the validated `base_patient_id`; query validation, `canManagePatientSharing`, org scoping, no-store headers, response redaction, POST create behavior, schema, migrations, frontend, external send, and destructive operations are unchanged.
- performance issues found: no DB query shape changed. The route now reuses the already-fetched visible page rows to compute small `Set` counts, and the persisted audit JSON is smaller.
- validation commands: `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.test.ts src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/__tests__/protected-post-routes.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `pnpm exec eslint src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.ts src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.test.ts`; `pnpm exec prettier --write src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.test.ts`; `pnpm exec prettier --check src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.ts src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.test.ts`; `git diff --check -- src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.ts src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.test.ts`; long-gate `pnpm typecheck` under token `314CA8D5-9DA0-4299-A875-10A3D465A7FF`.

### 20260625-2156 JST

- current task: follow up on the patient-share-cases privacy review by tightening `GET /api/patient-share-cases` from visit-only access to patient-sharing management access.
- files inspected: agmsg inbox; `git status --short --untracked-files=all`; `src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.ts`; `src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.test.ts`; `src/lib/api/route-catalog.ts`; `src/app/api/meta/route-catalog/route.test.ts`; `src/lib/auth/permission-matrix.ts`; `src/lib/auth/context.ts`; `src/lib/auth/__tests__/permissions.test.ts`; `src/app/(dashboard)/workflow/pharmacy-cooperation/pharmacy-cooperation-workflow-content.tsx`; `src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts`; privacy and API-contract subagent reports from the preceding patient-share-cases slice.
- files changed: `src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.ts`, `src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.test.ts`, `src/lib/api/route-catalog.ts`, `src/app/api/meta/route-catalog/route.test.ts`, and this Ralph state entry.
- bugs found: `GET /api/patient-share-cases` was guarded by `canVisit`, while neighboring pharmacy-cooperation list routes such as `GET /api/pharmacy-visit-requests` and `GET /api/partner-visit-records` are already `canManagePatientSharing`. This allowed visit-only roles such as `pharmacist_trainee` to enumerate org-wide patient-share-case metadata without patient-sharing lifecycle authority.
- security risks found: reduced least-privilege risk by requiring `canManagePatientSharing` for the patient-share-case list route and aligning the route catalog. The new route test proves a `pharmacist_trainee` receives `403 AUTH_FORBIDDEN` before `withOrgContext`, `patientShareCase.findMany`, or `patient_share_cases_viewed` audit writes. Existing `pharmacist`/`admin` access, query validation from the previous slice, no-store headers, response redaction, POST create permission, schema, migrations, frontend, external send, and destructive operations are unchanged.
- performance issues found: unauthorized visit-only roles now fail before DB and audit writes. Authorized list behavior remains unchanged.
- validation commands: `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/meta/route-catalog/route.test.ts src/lib/api/route-catalog.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `pnpm exec eslint src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.ts src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.test.ts src/lib/api/route-catalog.ts src/app/api/meta/route-catalog/route.test.ts`; `pnpm exec prettier --check src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.ts src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.test.ts src/lib/api/route-catalog.ts src/app/api/meta/route-catalog/route.test.ts`; `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.test.ts src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/__tests__/protected-post-routes.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `git diff --check -- src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.ts src/app/api/patient-share-cases/route.test.ts src/lib/api/route-catalog.ts src/app/api/meta/route-catalog/route.test.ts`; long-gate `pnpm typecheck` under token `DE804878-DE3B-4CC9-AF84-2AB47B378108`.
- validation results: focused patient-share-cases route Vitest passed `1` file / `27` tests; route-catalog suites passed `2` files / `6` tests; focused ESLint passed; focused Prettier check passed; patient-share-cases plus protected GET route suites passed `2` files / `156` tests; protected POST route matrix passed `1` file / `97` tests with the known webhook org-dispatch stderr; focused diff-check passed; `pnpm typecheck` passed and the local long-gate lock was released.
- remaining work: stage only the patient-share-cases route/test, route catalog files, and this Ralph state entry; commit the slice; send `DONE` to agmsg; then continue backend/API/security hardening. ### 20260625-2135 JST

- current task: continue Codex-only backend/API security hardening by adding local query bounds to the `/api/phos/*` same-origin proxy before upstream forwarding.
- files inspected: agmsg inbox; `git status --short --untracked-files=all`; local Next.js Route Handlers guide `node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/01-getting-started/15-route-handlers.md`; `src/app/api/phos/[...path]/route.ts`; `src/app/api/phos/[...path]/route.test.ts`; `src/phos/api/client.ts`; `src/phos/api/client.test.ts`; `src/phos/api/types.ts`; `src/phos/backend/input-validation.ts`; `src/phos/contracts/phos_contracts.ts`; `src/phos/infra/api-gateway-routes.ts`; gbrain call-graph outputs; security and API-contract subagent reports.
- files changed: `src/app/api/phos/[...path]/route.ts`, `src/app/api/phos/[...path]/route.test.ts`, `src/phos/api/client.test.ts`, and this Ralph state entry.
- bugs found: the local PHOS proxy authenticated and catalog-checked requests, then copied all incoming query pairs to the upstream URL without aggregate length, param-count, per-key, per-value, or duplicate-key bounds.
- security risks found: reduced authenticated resource-boundary/availability risk by rejecting oversized PHOS proxy query strings before constructing/fetching the upstream URL. The guard now caps encoded query length at 8192, param pairs at 32, encoded key length at 64, encoded value length at 2048, and rejects duplicate query keys because the typed PHOS client and backend query readers use single-value params. New local proxy validation errors use canonical PHOS `ErrorResponse` shape with no raw query values in details, so same-origin clients preserve the error instead of normalizing it to `INTERNAL_ERROR`. Existing auth token lookup, route catalog allowlist, upstream base URL normalization, forwarded header allowlist, redirect blocking, timeout, normal query forwarding, and response header allowlist are preserved.
- performance issues found: oversized query requests now fail before upstream URL append/fetch and before pushing large request targets into the PHOS upstream/API Gateway path. Normal typed-client queries remain unchanged.
- validation commands: `pnpm exec vitest run 'src/app/api/phos/[...path]/route.test.ts' src/phos/api/client.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `pnpm exec eslint 'src/app/api/phos/[...path]/route.ts' 'src/app/api/phos/[...path]/route.test.ts' src/phos/api/client.test.ts`; `pnpm exec prettier --check 'src/app/api/phos/[...path]/route.ts' 'src/app/api/phos/[...path]/route.test.ts' src/phos/api/client.test.ts`; `git diff --check -- 'src/app/api/phos/[...path]/route.ts' 'src/app/api/phos/[...path]/route.test.ts' src/phos/api/client.test.ts`; long-gate `pnpm typecheck` under token `EE9DA61B-DEFD-4EF4-9136-5878F2BB2263`.
- validation results: focused PHOS proxy/client Vitest passed `2` files / `55` tests; focused ESLint passed; focused Prettier check passed; focused diff-check passed; `pnpm typecheck` passed and the local long-gate lock was released. Security reviewer rated the missing bound Medium and suggested 8192/32/64/2048; API-contract reviewer confirmed client compatibility and requested canonical local proxy errors plus duplicate-key rejection, both implemented.
- remaining work: stage only the PHOS proxy/client test files plus this Ralph state entry, commit the slice, send `DONE` to agmsg, close reviewer subagents, and continue backend/API hardening. Leave untracked `.agent-loop/plans/*` untouched.
- next action: drain agmsg, run final scoped Prettier/diff-check including this Ralph state entry, and commit the PHOS proxy query bounds slice.

- current task: continue Codex-only backend/API privacy hardening by fail-closing explicit blank inquiry-record filters, adding sensitive no-store headers, and honoring explicit `limit` without truncating no-limit callers.
- files inspected: agmsg inbox; `git status --short --untracked-files=all`; local Next.js Route Handlers guide `node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/01-getting-started/15-route-handlers.md`; `src/app/api/inquiry-records/route.ts`; `src/app/api/inquiry-records/route.test.ts`; `src/lib/api/pagination.ts`; `src/lib/api/sensitive-response.ts`; direct `/api/inquiry-records` callers; protected GET catalog; gbrain `code_blast` and `code_callers` for `src/app/api/inquiry-records/route.ts::GET`; security-auditor, privacy-compliance, and medical-safety reviewer reports.
- files changed: `src/app/api/inquiry-records/route.ts`, `src/app/api/inquiry-records/route.test.ts`, and this Ralph state entry.
- bugs found: `GET /api/inquiry-records?patient_id=` and `?cycle_id=` treated explicitly blank clinical filters as omitted filters, widening the request to the org/assignment-scoped inquiry list. `status=` had the same explicit-empty ambiguity. The route also returned clinical inquiry content without sensitive no-store headers, and offered no bounded path for callers that want a capped list.
- security risks found: reduced PHI/clinical-content overfetch risk by rejecting present-but-empty `patient_id`, `cycle_id`, and `status` before DB access; added `withSensitiveNoStore` to 200 and validation-error responses. Existing `canVisit`, `org_id`, `buildMedicationCycleAssignmentWhere(ctx)`, status semantics, selected clinical fields, and POST side effects are preserved. No auth/authz permission semantics, RLS, DB schema, migration, billing, frontend, external send, or destructive behavior changed.
- performance issues found: explicit `limit` requests now apply `take: limit + 1`, trim the response to `limit`, and return additive `meta: { limit, has_more }`. After medical-safety review, no-limit calls intentionally keep the previous no-`take`, no-`meta` full-list behavior to avoid silently hiding older patient inquiry records from existing UI callers.

- files changed: `src/server/services/outbound-webhook.ts`, `src/server/services/outbound-webhook.test.ts`, and this Ralph state entry.
- bugs found: `dispatchWebhookEventForOrg` loaded every active webhook registration for an org, including registrations for unrelated events, then filtered by `registration.events.includes(event)` in memory. This avoided wrong dispatch but still read unrelated webhook URLs, secret fields, and event arrays for every event dispatch.
- security risks found: reduced unnecessary in-process exposure of unrelated active webhook registration secrets and URLs by adding a DB-level `events: { has: event }` filter while preserving `org_id` and `is_active` scope. The dispatch-time `registration.isActive && registration.events.includes(event)` filter remains as defense in depth, so legacy or unexpected rows still do not dispatch or persist delivery attempts for unsupported/nonmatching events. No schema, migration, frontend, auth/authz, retry behavior, external payload shape, URL validation, secret signing, or destructive behavior changed.
- performance issues found: reduced webhook dispatch registration reads from all active org registrations to active registrations subscribed to the requested event. This shrinks DB payload and Node filtering work on hot event dispatch paths.

PHI exposure is not expanded beyond the existing aggregate `patient_count`.
- performance issues found: added `parseBoundedInteger` with `DEFAULT_EXTERNAL_PROFESSIONAL_SEARCH_LIMIT = 500` and `MAX_EXTERNAL_PROFESSIONAL_SEARCH_LIMIT = 500`, applying `take: limit + 1` only when nonblank `q` is present. q-present responses return `items.slice(0, limit)` plus additive `meta: { limit, has_more }`; q-absent and blank-q requests intentionally have no `take`, no truncation, and no `meta` to preserve full selector completeness.

### 20260625-0959 JST

- current task: implement Codex-owned `F-20260625-staff-workload-recent-tasks-batch` after Claude `LOCK_GRANT`, covering `/api/staff-workload` route-level performance without frontend, schema, migration, service, or auth changes.
- files inspected: agmsg inbox/history for Claude's `F-20260625-claude-patient-status-tracker-perf` completion, `F-20260625-staff-workload-recent-tasks-batch` lock grant, and long-gate release; `git status --short --branch --untracked-files=all`; `src/app/api/staff-workload/route.ts`; `src/app/api/staff-workload/route.test.ts`; `prisma/schema/medication.prisma` `Task` model and enum/index definitions; `prisma/schema/prescription.prisma` related assigned-task indexes; existing raw SQL/window-query patterns in `src/server/services/patient-service.ts`, `src/app/api/visit-records/route.ts`, and `src/server/services/workflow-dashboard-queries.ts`; read-only performance reviewer output for staff workload N+1 risk; independent DB reviewer output for raw SQL null-ordering semantics; gbrain `code_callers` and `code_blast` output for `src/app/api/staff-workload/route.ts::GET`.
- files changed: `src/app/api/staff-workload/route.ts`, `src/app/api/staff-workload/route.test.ts`, and this Ralph state entry.
- bugs found: `/api/staff-workload` fetched recent open tasks with `mapWithConcurrency(staffIds, 4, prisma.task.findMany(... take: 4))`, issuing one query per active staff member. The test suite also encoded the staff-count query pattern by expecting two `task.findMany` calls for two staff.
- security risks found: no auth/authz, tenant, PHI projection, response shape, DB schema, migration, billing, deploy, or destructive behavior changed. The new raw query preserves `org_id = ctx.orgId`, restricts `assigned_to` to the active staff id set, and keeps open-task statuses limited to `pending` / `in_progress`. It uses Prisma tagged `$queryRaw`, not unsafe string interpolation.
- performance issues found: reduced recent-open-task DB round trips from active-staff count to one batched query. The route now uses `ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY assigned_to ORDER BY sla_due_at ASC NULLS LAST, due_date ASC NULLS LAST, priority ASC, created_at DESC)` and `rn <= 4` to keep the same per-staff maximum while preserving open task counts, visit lookup, dispense task grouping, date filtering, and workload scoring. An intermediate `NULLS FIRST` draft was rejected by Claude review because it would rank undated tasks ahead of dated SLA tasks and change the capped top-four semantics; the final query pins `NULLS LAST`.
- validation commands: focused `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/staff-workload/route.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; focused `pnpm exec eslint src/app/api/staff-workload/route.ts src/app/api/staff-workload/route.test.ts`; focused `pnpm exec prettier --check src/app/api/staff-workload/route.ts src/app/api/staff-workload/route.test.ts`; focused `git diff --check` for the two staff-workload files; long-gate-locked `pnpm typecheck`.
- validation results: focused staff-workload Vitest passed `3/3`; focused ESLint passed; focused Prettier passed; focused diff-check passed; `pnpm typecheck` passed under local long-gate lock after `next typegen` and both TypeScript projects completed successfully. After peer review, nullable ordering was corrected to explicit `NULLS LAST` and the focused validation bundle was rerun. Tests now assert `task.findMany` is not called, `$queryRaw` is called once with the org id and active staff ids, the SQL includes `ROW_NUMBER()`, `PARTITION BY assigned_to`, `sla_due_at ASC NULLS LAST`, `due_date ASC NULLS LAST`, `org_id =`, `assigned_to = ANY(...)`, `status IN ('pending', 'in_progress')`, and `WHERE rn <=`, and the response still caps each staff member's rendered open tasks at four.
- remaining work: send `READY_FOR_REVIEW` to Claude with validation evidence, wait for approval, then stage and commit only the two staff-workload files plus `.codex/ralph-state.md`. Migration/index follow-ups remain separate and human-gated if schema changes are proposed.
- next action: drain agmsg, run final formatting/diff checks including this ledger entry, send `READY_FOR_REVIEW`, handle Claude feedback, and commit after approval.

- bugs found: three API route catch blocks dumped raw `Error` objects with `console.error` on job/flush failure. The HTTP response was already generic, but process logs could still include provider error messages, stack traces, or secret-like details from CloudWatch/job handlers.
- security risks found: reduced log-leak risk by replacing the raw `console.error(Error)` calls with the existing `logger.error({ ...safeContext }, err)` overload, which emits a structured event with `error_name` while omitting `Error.message` and `stack`. The patch deliberately does not touch `src/server/jobs/runner.ts`, persisted job `error_log`, `/admin/jobs`, job success payload `errors`, auth/authz, tenant scope, DB schema, migrations, external calls, billing, deploy, or destructive behavior. Broader `/admin/jobs` persisted error-log redaction remains a human-gated `BLOCKED.md` item.
- performance issues found: no runtime performance path changed. The patch adds one safe logger call per failure path and only affects error logging; fetch/job execution counts, authentication, side effects, and response payloads are preserved. Read-only follow-up candidate found: `/api/staff-workload` recent-task lookup has staff-count N+1 queries and can likely be batched with a route-only PostgreSQL window query, with migration/index work deferred separately.
- validation commands: focused `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/jobs/flush-metrics/route.test.ts 'src/app/api/jobs/[jobType]/route.test.ts' src/app/api/admin/flush-metrics/route.test.ts src/lib/utils/logger.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; focused `pnpm exec eslint` for the six API route/test files; focused `pnpm exec prettier --check` for the six API route/test files; focused `git diff --check` for the six API route/test files; `rg` scan confirming only `logger.error` remains under the touched job/flush routes; long-gate-locked `pnpm typecheck`.
- validation results: focused Vitest passed `36/36`; focused ESLint passed; focused Prettier passed; focused diff-check passed; route scan found no remaining direct `console.error` in the touched job/flush routes; `pnpm typecheck` passed under local long-gate lock after `next typegen` and both TypeScript projects completed successfully. After an independent reviewer noted that console-error spies should restore even if an assertion fails, the three failure tests were tightened with `try/finally` cleanup and the focused Vitest, ESLint, Prettier, diff-check, and long-gate `pnpm typecheck` bundle passed again. Tests now assert the failure responses remain generic and the actual structured log output does not contain provider detail, `stack`, or `error_message`.
- remaining work: wait for Claude to confirm that approval still stands after the test-only `try/finally` cleanup, then commit only the six API files plus `.codex/ralph-state.md` if approved. After this slice, continue backend-foundation work instead of new frontend UIUX slices. Candidate backlog: `staff-workload` N+1 route-only batching, outbound webhook SSRF redirect/DNS-rebinding hardening, and human-gated escalations for shared `JOB_API_KEY` all-tenant job scope, data-explorer security metadata editing, and set-photo attachment boundaries.

- bugs found: the schedules day board mixed all-time workload counters into a date-scoped UI: completed dispense audit tasks were counted across the org instead of the current day's schedule cycles, and report-pending count used an all-time `medicationCycle.count` for `visit_completed` cycles. The page also issued a separate `/api/tasks?status=open&task_types=...` request, then filtered org-wide task results client-side to the visible board.
- security risks found: F-022 reduces schedule-board task overexposure by moving operational task retrieval into the day-board BFF, applying the existing dashboard assignment scope, filtering to current visible visit/proposal entity ids, selecting only bounded presentation fields, and serializing `metadata: null`. Tests assert task metadata and memo-like values are not exposed through the schedule board response. No auth, RLS, DB schema, migration, API mutation semantics, audit log, secret, billing/payment, deploy, or destructive operation changed.
- performance issues found: F-022 removes the extra initial org-wide `/api/tasks` fetch from the schedule board, replaces the all-time report count with a count derived from already-fetched day schedules, scopes completed dispense audit grouping to current-day cycle ids, and limits operational task retrieval to at most 24 current-board tasks. The frontend now renders the BFF-provided `operational_tasks` field and keeps the existing task status mutation path.
- validation commands: focused `pnpm exec vitest run 'src/app/api/visit-schedules/day-board/route.test.ts' 'src/app/(dashboard)/schedules/schedule-team-board.test.tsx' --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; focused ESLint for the five F-022 files; focused Prettier check; focused `git diff --check`; Claude CODE_REVIEW; isolated combined F-022+F-023 long gate using `next typegen`, `tsc --noEmit`, service-worker `tsc`, `next build --webpack`, and no-unused `tsc` commands from the main `node_modules` against a temporary worktree.
- validation results: focused schedule day-board Vitest passed `28/28`; focused ESLint, Prettier, and diff-check passed. The isolated combined long gate for F-022 plus Claude's F-023 dashboard cockpit time-zone fix passed typegen + `tsc` + service-worker `tsc`, `next build --webpack`, and no-unused `tsc` + service-worker no-unused. Claude approved F-022 after independent review, confirming the current-day workload scoping, BFF task aggregation, assignment-scope use, metadata stripping, and removal of the initial org-wide `/api/tasks` query.
- remaining work: commit only the five F-022 source/test/type files plus this ledger entry, release the F-022 locks, and leave Claude-owned F-024 drug-masters changes plus persistent untracked `ops/**` / `projects/careviax/**` artifacts unstaged. Raw holiday ids remain preserved for React Query keys, editing/delete state, request bodies, POST collection path semantics, invalidation keys, and API route identity. No auth, RLS, DB schema, migration, API body contract, PHI projection, logging, secret, billing/payment, deploy, or destructive operation changed.
- performance issues found: no runtime performance path changed. The patch adds small shared-helper calls and hoists one bulk JSON header object outside the per-date loop while preserving fetch count per selected date, query keys, invalidation fan-out, payload shape, and rendered UI structure.

- bugs found: preview `print-audit` responses could return a schema-valid report body for a different report id and still be rendered before the final `print_requested` guard; print-audit accepted non-printable confirmed report types; nullable report content was not fail-closed before audit persistence; `/api/tasks/bulk` response/failure DTOs were duplicated between route and UI; malformed successful bulk responses could be normalized into apparent success; the print-audit route's second access-check failure path lacked direct coverage; `PatientWorkspaceRail`, `PharmacistMemoTab`, and the `PatientDocumentsPanel` wrapper were stale unimported compatibility components while `FirstVisitDocumentsPanel` is the current used surface.
- security risks found: wrong-report preview rendering could expose or print the wrong medical report content if a malformed 2xx preview response was accepted. The fix now requires matching report id for both preview and final print intents, restricts printable report types, and blocks null content before audit persistence. Bulk task failure summaries remain sanitized and do not display task ids or patient values.
- performance issues found: no DB query shape, mutation batching, rendering loop, or network call count changed. Added Zod parsing is limited to small print-audit and bulk-completion response bodies.

### 20260620-0153 JST

- current task: harden patient-share-consent revoke so the high-risk consent撤回 action requires a reason and a `ConfirmDialog` before calling the revoke API.
- files inspected: `docs/ui-ux-design-guidelines.md`, `src/app/(dashboard)/workflow/pharmacy-cooperation/pharmacy-cooperation-workflow-content.tsx`, `src/app/(dashboard)/workflow/pharmacy-cooperation/pharmacy-cooperation-workflow-content.test.tsx`, accessibility reviewer findings, validation output, and current git status.
- files changed: `src/app/(dashboard)/workflow/pharmacy-cooperation/pharmacy-cooperation-workflow-content.tsx`, `src/app/(dashboard)/workflow/pharmacy-cooperation/pharmacy-cooperation-workflow-content.test.tsx`, `CODEX_GOAL_PROGRESS.md`, and this Ralph state entry.
- bugs found: patient-share-consent revoke accepted a reason input but the revoke button was only disabled by busy state, used a non-destructive variant, and called the revoke mutation directly. That allowed accidental or reasonless consent撤回 attempts without the shared confirmation gate used by other pharmacy-cooperation state transitions. The initial concurrent change also used the current selected share-case ID in the revoke URL; this was fixed to use the consent's own `share_case_id` captured in the pending action.
- security risks found: reduced consent/PHI-sharing governance risk by requiring a trimmed revoke reason, forcing a destructive confirmation dialog with consent ID/date/share-case/pharmacy context, making the mutation reject empty reasons before sending any request body, and binding the request to the consent's share-case ID. No auth, permission, DB schema, PHI projection, message content, or unrelated workflow API behavior changed.
- performance issues found: no query, polling, invalidation, or render performance issue was found or changed. The update is local state-gating and one extra confirmation step before the existing revoke mutation.
- validation commands: `pnpm exec prettier --write 'src/app/(dashboard)/workflow/pharmacy-cooperation/pharmacy-cooperation-workflow-content.tsx' 'src/app/(dashboard)/workflow/pharmacy-cooperation/pharmacy-cooperation-workflow-content.test.tsx' tools/tests/ui-route-mocked-smoke.spec.ts`; `pnpm exec eslint 'src/app/(dashboard)/workflow/pharmacy-cooperation/pharmacy-cooperation-workflow-content.tsx' 'src/app/(dashboard)/workflow/pharmacy-cooperation/pharmacy-cooperation-workflow-content.test.tsx' tools/tests/ui-route-mocked-smoke.spec.ts`; `pnpm exec vitest run 'src/app/(dashboard)/workflow/pharmacy-cooperation/pharmacy-cooperation-workflow-content.test.tsx' --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `pnpm typecheck`; `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm format:check`; `git diff --check`; focused `rg` scan for revoke wiring.
- validation results: targeted Prettier passed unchanged. Targeted ESLint passed. Initial focused Vitest failed because the confirmation detail shortened the consent ID; the detail was changed to show the full consent ID, then focused Vitest passed with 1 file / 12 tests. After adding share-case ID binding, focused Vitest passed again with 1 file / 12 tests. `pnpm typecheck` passed. `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm format:check` passed. `git diff --check` passed. The focused scan confirmed the revoke mutation is now only invoked through the pending workflow action path and still rejects empty reasons in the mutation function.
- remaining work: pharmacy-cooperation UI/UX remediation remains active. Browser/a11y proof was expanded in the next slice. Remaining high/medium candidates include patient-link acceptance context and repeated workflow/admin row action names.
- next action: commit workflow consent-revoke implementation/test, commit browser proof test, then commit ledger separately.

### 20260620-0011 JST

- current task: centralize patient-share data output scope policy for v0.2 R2.
- files inspected: attached v0.2 R2 output/attachment permission requirements, `docs/pharmacy-cooperation-v0.2-completion-audit.md`, `Plans.md`, `src/server/services/patient-share-policy.ts`, `src/server/services/patient-share-policy.test.ts`, `src/server/services/patient-share-scope.ts`, `src/server/services/patient-share-access.ts`, `src/server/services/file-download-audit.ts`, relevant file-storage/download context, and validation output.

- bugs found: v0.2 share scopes already contained `attachments`, `print`, `pdf_output`, and `download`, but the R2 rule mapping output actions to required scope keys was not centralized. That left future attachment/PDF/print/download routes at risk of interpreting the same share scope differently.
- security risks found: reduced output-permission drift by adding fail-closed shared policy for attachment view, attachment download, print, PDF output, PDF download, and data download actions. Attachment downloads require both `attachments` and `download`; PDF downloads require both `pdf_output` and `download`; inactive share cases are denied even when scope keys are enabled. No API, DB schema, PHI projection, notification, or route behavior was changed in this slice.
- performance issues found: no DB query, network call, render path, or loop was added. The policy uses in-memory scope-key normalization on already-loaded share-case data.

- validation results: targeted Prettier passed. Focused Vitest passed with 1 file / 8 tests. Targeted ESLint passed. `pnpm typecheck` passed.
- remaining work: direct DB-backed proof and migration application remain explicit-approval blocked. Concrete output routes can be wired to this policy where share-case context is available, and broader role-matrix browser proof remains after DB apply.

- bugs found: DB-backed patient-card share-case creation failed with Prisma `Unknown argument org_id` because `POST /api/patient-share-cases` passed an explicit `org_id` inside nested `patient_link.create`. Prisma infers the child org from the parent `PatientShareCase` composite relation, so the nested create must omit it.
- security risks found: no new PHI surface was introduced. The new browser proof verifies only operational IDs/status/version/link count from the local e2e DB, and the API response path remains PHI-minimized without patient snapshots, names, addresses, or raw share scope in the response.

- bugs found: real DB-backed invoice draft creation failed under Prisma 7 because `createPharmacyInvoiceDraft` passed `org_id` inside nested `PharmacyInvoiceItem` create data. - bugs found: `POST /api/patient-share-cases` performed same-transaction validation reads through `Promise.all`, and `POST /api/patient-share-cases/:id/activate` used a nested relation `include` on an interactive transaction update. - security risks found: activation previously returned full `patient_link` include data after update, which could expose patient-link snapshots/identity proof in a mutation response. The response is now no-store and minimized to safe status/link/partnership fields with `has_partner_patient_id` instead of raw snapshots.
