- files changed: `src/app/(dashboard)/reports/print/print-hub-content.tsx`, `src/app/(dashboard)/reports/print/print-hub-content.test.tsx`, `CODEX_GOAL_PROGRESS.md`, and this Ralph state entry.
- bugs found: print hub's visit-report print-audit query key was scoped only by org/report id, so React Query could expose cached audited body text while a new `preview_rendered` POST was pending or failing. The print button disabled state also only checked pending/error, not whether the current audit response had `audited === true` and a report payload.
- security risks found: PHI/medical report preview could be displayed from stale cached audit data before the current preview audit succeeded. The fix gates report body rendering and printing on the current audit run and keeps the existing fail-closed API behavior.
- performance issues found: no material runtime cost. The query key gains a per-mount nonce and the same single preview audit POST still runs for visit-report previews.
- validation commands: `pnpm exec prettier --write 'src/app/(dashboard)/reports/print/print-hub-content.tsx' 'src/app/(dashboard)/reports/print/print-hub-content.test.tsx'`; `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=16384 pnpm exec vitest run 'src/app/(dashboard)/reports/print/print-hub-content.test.tsx' --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=16384 pnpm exec vitest run src/lib/reports/care-report-print-audit-contract.test.ts 'src/app/api/care-reports/[id]/print-audit/route.test.ts' 'src/app/(dashboard)/reports/[id]/print/page.test.tsx' 'src/app/(dashboard)/reports/print/print-hub-content.test.tsx' --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `pnpm exec eslint 'src/app/(dashboard)/reports/print/print-hub-content.tsx' 'src/app/(dashboard)/reports/print/print-hub-content.test.tsx'`; `pnpm typecheck`; `pnpm typecheck:no-unused`; `git diff --check -- 'src/app/(dashboard)/reports/print/print-hub-content.tsx' 'src/app/(dashboard)/reports/print/print-hub-content.test.tsx'`.

### 20260622-1931 JST

- current task: codex-owned F-20260622-006 patient MCS query URL path-segment hardening.
- files inspected: agmsg inbox output, `src/lib/patient-mcs/query.ts`, `src/lib/patient-mcs/query.test.ts`, related patient MCS UI route usages found by code-mapper, `.agent-loop/REVIEW_LOG.md`, `.agent-loop/VERIFY_LOG.md`, and this Ralph state file.
- files changed: `src/lib/patient-mcs/query.ts`, `src/lib/patient-mcs/query.test.ts`, `.agent-loop/REVIEW_LOG.md`, `.agent-loop/VERIFY_LOG.md`, and this Ralph state entry.
- bugs found: `fetchPatientMcsOverview` interpolated raw `patientId` into `/api/patients/${patientId}/mcs`, so a hostile or malformed id containing `/`, `?`, or `#` could change the requested route/query/fragment. Fixed by encoding only the path segment with `encodeURIComponent(patientId)` while preserving raw query keys for cache identity.
- security risks found: reduced a defense-in-depth path traversal / route confusion risk in the patient MCS fetch helper. No auth, billing, payments, RLS, DB, migration, production deploy, secret, cookie, PHI logging, or destructive behavior was touched. Claude confirmed this is not a §15 hard-stop because server-side authz/normalization remains the primary boundary.
- performance issues found: no material runtime performance change; `encodeURIComponent` is constant-time relative to the existing single fetch path and does not alter cache keys or request fan-out.
- validation commands: baseline `pnpm exec vitest run src/lib/patient-mcs/query.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; focused final Vitest for the same file; scoped `pnpm exec eslint --max-warnings=0 src/lib/patient-mcs/query.ts src/lib/patient-mcs/query.test.ts`; scoped `pnpm exec prettier --check src/lib/patient-mcs/query.ts src/lib/patient-mcs/query.test.ts`; scoped `git diff --check -- src/lib/patient-mcs/query.ts src/lib/patient-mcs/query.test.ts`; `pnpm typecheck`; `pnpm build`; `pnpm typecheck:no-unused`.
- validation results: baseline Vitest 6/6 passed; final focused Vitest 7/7 passed; scoped ESLint, Prettier, and diff-check passed; `pnpm typecheck` passed; `pnpm build` passed after removing stale `.next/dev/lock` with no live Next build holder; `pnpm typecheck:no-unused` passed serially after build. Claude PATCH_REVIEW_RESULT approved rev1.
- remaining work: stage only the two patient-mcs files plus F-006 ledger rows, commit, send DONE/release for F-006 source and ledger locks, then return to autonomous next-candidate intake. UI direct MCS path interpolations are intentionally deferred to Claude-owned follow-up (`F-20260622-007` candidate) because they live under `src/app/(dashboard)/**` / `src/components/**`.

- bugs found: `derivePatientBoardCard` generated browser-facing board card patient hrefs with raw `/patients/${patient.id}` interpolation. - files changed: `src/server/services/patient-detail.ts`, `src/server/services/patient-detail.test.ts`, `src/server/services/patient-detail-tasks.ts`, `src/server/services/patient-detail-tasks.test.ts`, `src/server/services/patient-detail-timeline-events.ts`, `src/server/services/patient-detail-timeline-query.test.ts`, `src/app/api/patients/[id]/detail-slices.test.ts`, `prisma/schema/admin.prisma`, `prisma/schema/medication.prisma`, `prisma/migrations/20260624063000_patient_timeline_backend_hardening/migration.sql`, and this Ralph state entry.
- bugs found: `getPatientTimelineData` had an unbounded first-visit document timeline query, fail-fast timeline source fan-out that could turn one source outage into a full timeline 500, server-timezone-sensitive date labels that could show the wrong JST day/month, and nondeterministic ordering for same-timestamp cross-source timeline events. The AuditLog operation-history query also lacked a target-scoped composite index, but adding `patient_id` to the predicate would have dropped legacy/export audit rows where `patient_id` is null.
- security risks found: reduced audit/timeline performance and availability risk by bounding first-visit documents, adding additive target/date indexes, making only the timeline source batch fail-soft, and keeping legacy target-scoped AuditLog filters without a lossy `patient_id` predicate. Partial failure logging is redacted to `{ orgId, source, error: error.name }`, and the client-facing `partial_failures` message is generic. Did not wrap the long fan-out in one `withOrgContext` interactive transaction because that would reintroduce timeout risk and defeat fail-soft behavior; explicit `org_id` filters remain the enforced isolation for this pass. Prod migration apply remains human-gated.
- performance issues found: added `@@index([org_id, target_type, target_id, created_at(sort: Desc)])` for AuditLog timeline operation history and `@@index([org_id, patient_id, created_at(sort: Desc)])` for FirstVisitDocument timeline reads; added `take: 8` to first-visit documents; raised timeline-only source concurrency to 8; overlapped source actor-name resolution with the AuditLog query. No cursor pagination or source-adapter registry was bundled.
- validation commands: focused `pnpm exec vitest run 'src/app/api/patients/[id]/detail-slices.test.ts' src/server/services/patient-detail-tasks.test.ts src/server/services/patient-detail-timeline-query.test.ts src/server/services/patient-detail.test.ts`; scoped `pnpm exec eslint` on changed TS/TSX files; scoped `pnpm exec prettier --check` on changed TS/TSX files after formatting; scoped `git diff --check`; `pnpm exec prisma validate`; local e2e `DATABASE_URL=...localhost:5433... pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec prisma migrate deploy --schema=prisma/schema/`; local e2e `prisma migrate status`; node `pg` query against local e2e `pg_indexes`; `pnpm typecheck`; `pnpm typecheck:no-unused`; Claude high-risk CODE_REVIEW.
- validation results: focused Vitest passed 4 files / 106 tests; scoped ESLint passed; scoped Prettier passed; scoped diff-check passed; Prisma schema validate passed; local e2e migration deploy applied `20260624063000_patient_timeline_backend_hardening`; migrate status reported up to date; node `pg` confirmed `AuditLog_org_target_created_idx` and `FirstVisitDocument_org_patient_created_idx`; `pnpm typecheck` passed; `pnpm typecheck:no-unused` passed. ### 20260625-1027 JST

- current task: codex-owned backend hardening pivot for F-20260625-prescription-intakes-qr-null-mismatch, after closing F-20260625-today-preparation-audit-queue-take-one as a false-positive with Claude.
- files inspected: agmsg inbox/history for Claude grants and ACKs, `.agent-loop/research/workflow-findings-20260625.md`, `node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/03-api-reference/03-file-conventions/route.md`, `src/app/api/visits/today-preparation/route.ts`, `src/app/api/visits/today-preparation/route.test.ts`, `prisma/schema/prescription.prisma`, `src/app/api/admin/organizations/route.ts`, `src/app/api/care-reports/route.ts`, `src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.ts`, `src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts`, gbrain refs/call graph outputs, and read-only subagent findings.
- files changed: `src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.ts`, `src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts`, and this Ralph state entry.
- bugs found: the QR draft line mismatch guard compared a submitted line only when the draft side had a non-null value. If the draft omitted an optional field such as `drugCode` but the submitted request supplied `drug_code`, the mismatch could be skipped and the QR draft could be claimed into an intake despite the request/draft line not matching.
- security risks found: reduced medication-data integrity and patient-safety risk by making QR draft line confirmation compare the normalized request and draft values bidirectionally. Empty-vs-empty still matches, but either side having a value while the other side is absent now blocks confirmation before `qrScanDraft.updateMany`, intake creation, realtime broadcast, and webhook side effects. No auth/RLS/schema/service/UI, production deploy, secret, cookie, PHI logging, or destructive behavior changed.
- performance issues found: no meaningful runtime performance change. The route still compares the same bounded per-line fields and only removes the one-sided skip that caused false negatives. Separately, the proposed `today-preparation` `dispenseTask.findMany take:10 -> take:1` perf slice was rejected as unsafe because the route post-filters latest audit result and then re-sorts narcotic tasks before returning `next_action`.
- validation commands: focused `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; scoped `pnpm exec eslint --max-warnings=0 src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.ts src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts`; scoped `pnpm exec prettier --check .codex/ralph-state.md src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.ts src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts`; scoped `git diff --check -- .codex/ralph-state.md src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.ts src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts`; long-gate `pnpm typecheck`.
- validation results: focused prescription-intakes route Vitest passed 1 file / 32 tests after adding no-side-effect assertions for both QR line mismatch tests; scoped ESLint passed; scoped Prettier passed; scoped diff-check passed; long-gate `pnpm typecheck` passed after the final assertion update. Claude granted the route-only lock and confirmed the today-preparation auditQueue item as a false-positive. Code-mapper subagent independently prioritized the QR mismatch route-only bug; data-integrity subagent independently confirmed the today-preparation `take:1` change would be behavior-breaking. Privacy review found one low test-coverage gap for `notifyWorkflowMutation` side effects, which was closed; medical-safety review found no issues and confirmed the stricter draft/request comparison is safety-positive. Claude CODE_REVIEW_RESULT approved rev2 and independently ran 66 route tests/teeth, with only a non-blocking follow-up suggestion for a positive both-sides-absent `drug_code` happy path.
- remaining work: drain agmsg, run final scoped ledger checks, stage only the two prescription-intakes files plus this Ralph entry, commit the approved slice, and notify Claude.
- next action: commit the approved route-only slice, keep untracked plan artifacts out of staging, then request/lock the handed-off `handoff-board/items` `is_active` route fix as the next Codex route slice.

### 20260625-1159 JST

- current task: codex-owned F-20260625-care-reports-case-org-recheck route hardening, after committing F-20260625-prescription-intakes-qr-null-mismatch as `dd30e0fa`.
- files inspected: agmsg inbox/history for Claude handoff and GRANT, `node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/03-api-reference/03-file-conventions/route.md`, `src/app/api/care-reports/route.ts`, `src/app/api/care-reports/route.test.ts`, gbrain call-graph/blast outputs, and read-only security reviewer output.
- files changed: `src/app/api/care-reports/route.ts`, `src/app/api/care-reports/route.test.ts`, and this Ralph state entry.
- bugs found: after `validateCareReportSource` resolved a case id, the POST path reused that id for `careReport.create`. The later enrichment lookup checked only `id` and `org_id`, treated a missing row as optional, and still created the report. A stale/deleted/reassigned case between validation and create could therefore leave a report linked to a no-longer-valid case id or fail open to a DB/runtime error instead of returning a validation response.
- security risks found: reduced patient/case tenant-boundary defense-in-depth risk by rechecking the resolved case with `id`, `org_id`, and `patient_id` immediately before enrichment/create, and by returning the existing generic case/patient validation error before `withOrgContext` or `careReport.create` side effects. No PHI is added to logs or error payloads, and patient-only reports with no resolved case id remain allowed.
- performance issues found: no meaningful runtime performance change. The existing enrichment lookup is kept but made fail-closed and patient-scoped; no new fan-out, pagination, schema, service, or UI work was introduced.
- validation commands: focused `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/care-reports/route.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; scoped `pnpm exec eslint --max-warnings=0 src/app/api/care-reports/route.ts src/app/api/care-reports/route.test.ts`; scoped `pnpm exec prettier --check .codex/ralph-state.md src/app/api/care-reports/route.ts src/app/api/care-reports/route.test.ts` after formatting; scoped `git diff --check -- .codex/ralph-state.md src/app/api/care-reports/route.ts src/app/api/care-reports/route.test.ts`; long-gate `pnpm typecheck`.
- validation results: focused care-reports route Vitest passed 1 file / 40 tests; scoped ESLint passed; scoped Prettier passed after formatting the test file; scoped diff-check passed; long-gate `pnpm typecheck` passed. Security reviewer confirmed the fail-closed guard is appropriate and noted this current checkout now has the intended guard in place. Privacy reviewer found no PHI/PII exposure, tenant/access-scope expansion, validation-before-write regression, or response-shape regression.
- remaining work: send READY_FOR_REVIEW to Claude, then commit only the two care-reports files plus this Ralph entry if approved.
- next action: submit the slice to Claude reviewer-audit and wait for approval before commit.

### 20260625-1320 JST

- current task: codex-owned F-20260625-outbound-webhook-no-redirect SSRF hardening after Claude finished day-board handoff and granted the outbound-webhook service/test lock.
- files inspected: agmsg inbox/history for Claude GRANT, CODE_REVIEW_RESULT approval, and §15-adjacent constraints, `src/server/services/outbound-webhook.ts`, `src/server/services/outbound-webhook.test.ts`, gbrain definition/reference outputs for `dispatchToEndpoint`, security reviewer output, strict reviewer output, and current git status.
- files changed: `src/server/services/outbound-webhook.ts`, `src/server/services/outbound-webhook.test.ts`, and this Ralph state entry.
- bugs found: outbound webhook dispatch validated the registered HTTPS URL before sending, but the actual `fetch` used default redirect behavior. A public, allowed webhook endpoint could return a 30x redirect to a private/link-local/internal URL after validation, creating a post-validation SSRF bypass.
- security risks found: reduced webhook redirect SSRF risk by setting `redirect: 'manual'` on the dispatch fetch. Redirect responses now remain ordinary non-OK delivery failures with the original registered URL and status code; the redirect `Location` is not followed, persisted, or returned. Security review found no blocker in this diff, but identified a remaining pre-existing DNS rebinding class where validation DNS and fetch connection DNS can differ; that needs a separate transport/resolver hardening slice.
- performance issues found: no meaningful runtime performance change. No new DB reads, loops, retries, or concurrency changes were introduced.

- bugs found: `GET /api/patient-self-reports` treated explicitly empty `patient_id` and `status` filters as omitted, allowing `?patient_id=` or `?status=%20%20` to widen to the caller's accessible self-report list. `PATCH /api/patient-self-reports/:id` accepted payloads containing only `updated_at`, allowing a no-op guarded update and audit entry with `changed_fields: []`.
- security risks found: reduced PHI caching risk by wrapping list/create/detail/update success and handled validation/not-found/conflict responses in `withSensitiveNoStore`, covering self-report content, reporter name/relation, callback preference, patient id, status, and masked clerk responses. Blank-present list filters now fail before patient/report queries, while omitted filters and padded valid values preserve current behavior.
- performance issues found: blank-present filters and no-op PATCH payloads now fail before DB reads/transactions, avoiding accidental broad list reads and unnecessary optimistic-lock update/audit work. No new DB reads, schema changes, or N+1 paths were introduced.
- validation commands: focused `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/patient-self-reports/route.test.ts 'src/app/api/patient-self-reports/[id]/route.test.ts' --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; scoped `pnpm exec eslint src/app/api/patient-self-reports/route.ts 'src/app/api/patient-self-reports/[id]/route.ts' src/app/api/patient-self-reports/route.test.ts 'src/app/api/patient-self-reports/[id]/route.test.ts'`; scoped `pnpm exec prettier --write src/app/api/patient-self-reports/route.ts 'src/app/api/patient-self-reports/[id]/route.ts' src/app/api/patient-self-reports/route.test.ts 'src/app/api/patient-self-reports/[id]/route.test.ts'`; scoped `pnpm exec prettier --check src/app/api/patient-self-reports/route.ts 'src/app/api/patient-self-reports/[id]/route.ts' src/app/api/patient-self-reports/route.test.ts 'src/app/api/patient-self-reports/[id]/route.test.ts'`; scoped `git diff --check -- src/app/api/patient-self-reports/route.ts 'src/app/api/patient-self-reports/[id]/route.ts' src/app/api/patient-self-reports/route.test.ts 'src/app/api/patient-self-reports/[id]/route.test.ts'`; long-gate `pnpm typecheck`.
- validation results: focused patient-self-reports Vitest passed 2 files / 30 tests, including blank-present GET rejection, `Cache-Control` + `Pragma` assertions for list/create/detail/PATCH success and handled error/conflict paths, and no-op PATCH rejection before DB access; scoped ESLint passed; scoped Prettier passed after formatting; scoped diff-check passed; `pnpm typecheck` passed under long-gate token `0E31CB98-3043-4597-82DC-FD4C6359E3BC`. One earlier typecheck attempt was intentionally interrupted by the user; a follow-up process/lock check found no running typecheck process and no remaining long-gate lock before the successful retry.

- security risks found: reduced PHI/operational-dashboard caching and caller-intent drift risk by rejecting malformed `view` before assignment-scope resolution, workflow cache reads, and dashboard aggregate service/DB reads; wrapping the authenticated GET handler with `withSensitiveNoStore`; preserving no-query `full` behavior and exact `view=full|phase|realtime|performance` compatibility; and extending protected GET no-store plus high-risk/meta route catalog coverage for workflow. Validation errors use fixed messages and do not echo raw query values. Auth, org scoping, assignment scoping, cache key shape, schema, migrations, external sends, and destructive operations were not changed.
- performance issues found: malformed `view` now fails before assignment-scope DB lookup, `serverCache.get`, and full/phase/realtime/performance workflow aggregate reads. No new list reads, heavier projections, or N+1 paths were introduced.
- validation commands: `pnpm prettier --write src/app/api/dashboard/workflow/route.ts src/app/api/dashboard/workflow/route.test.ts src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts src/lib/api/route-catalog.test.ts src/app/api/meta/route-catalog/route.test.ts`; focused `pnpm vitest run src/app/api/dashboard/workflow/route.test.ts src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts src/lib/api/route-catalog.test.ts src/app/api/meta/route-catalog/route.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; focused `pnpm eslint src/app/api/dashboard/workflow/route.ts src/app/api/dashboard/workflow/route.test.ts src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts src/lib/api/route-catalog.test.ts src/app/api/meta/route-catalog/route.test.ts`; focused `pnpm prettier --check ...`; focused `git diff --check -- ...`; long-gate `pnpm typecheck` under tokens `9AE5F365-80BC-4EFC-9739-0F76EEEAECD5` and final `5F562A06-2C3D-48F0-8266-FAC154695E31`; read-only privacy compliance reviewer; read-only API contract reviewer.
- validation results: focused dashboard workflow/protected GET/catalog Vitest passed `4` files / `189` tests after adding no-store assertions, malformed/duplicate `view` rejection before cache/aggregate reads, explicit `view=full` compatibility coverage, and protected GET/route-catalog coverage. ### 20260627-1358 JST

- current task: `/api/pharmacists` GET sensitive-list no-store hardening and sanitized unexpected-error response.
- files inspected: agmsg inbox for `phos/codex`, `git status --short --untracked-files=all`, `git diff` for the pharmacist slice, `node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/01-getting-started/15-route-handlers.md`, `node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/03-api-reference/04-functions/unstable_rethrow.md`, `src/app/api/pharmacists/route.ts`, `src/app/api/pharmacists/route.test.ts`, `src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts`, related sensitive-response patterns, and gbrain caller/blast checks for `src/app/api/pharmacists/route::GET`.
- files changed: `src/app/api/pharmacists/route.ts`, `src/app/api/pharmacists/route.test.ts`, `src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts`, `CODEX_GOAL_PROGRESS.md`, and this Ralph state entry.
- bugs found: `GET /api/pharmacists` returned pharmacist/staff listing data without the sensitive no-store cache policy on success, validation, forbidden, and unexpected-error paths. The unexpected-error path also relied on the generic auth wrapper boundary instead of this route's local sanitized `internalError()` body plus explicit no-store headers.
- security risks found: reduced PHI/staff-list cache retention risk by wrapping all authenticated GET responses in `withSensitiveNoStore()`. Added a sanitized 500 regression that confirms a thrown raw staff secret is not reflected in the response body. No auth, permission, org scoping, query filtering, POST behavior, audit logging, DB mutation, schema, migration, external send, or destructive operation changed.
- performance issues found: no new DB reads, loops, network calls, polling, subscriptions, or dependencies were introduced. The wrapper only mutates response headers and creates a sanitized fallback response on unexpected exceptions.
- validation commands: baseline `pnpm vitest run src/app/api/pharmacists/route.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `pnpm exec prettier --write src/app/api/pharmacists/route.ts src/app/api/pharmacists/route.test.ts src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts`; `pnpm vitest run src/app/api/pharmacists/route.test.ts src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `pnpm exec eslint src/app/api/pharmacists/route.ts src/app/api/pharmacists/route.test.ts src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts`; `pnpm exec prettier --check src/app/api/pharmacists/route.ts src/app/api/pharmacists/route.test.ts src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts`; `git diff --check -- src/app/api/pharmacists/route.ts src/app/api/pharmacists/route.test.ts src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts`; `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false --incremental false --project tsconfig.json`; `pnpm typecheck:no-unused`.
- validation results: baseline pharmacist route test passed `1` file / `19` tests. Focused pharmacist + protected GET matrix passed `2` files / `338` tests; the sanitized 500 case emitted the expected redacted route-handler log without leaking `raw pharmacist staff secret`. Scoped ESLint, Prettier check, and diff whitespace check passed. Full `tsc --noEmit` passed. Full `typecheck:no-unused` passed. Claude-owned `/admin/incidents` files were not edited by Codex; full type gates passed with the shared tree state.
- remaining work: run final ledger-aware scoped Prettier/diff/status, commit the pharmacist implementation group, then commit this progress ledger update separately. Send Claude a `PATCH_REVIEW_REQUEST` with commit hashes and validation results. The broader API sensitive-list no-store sweep remains incomplete.

### 20260628-1950 JST

- current task: add backend barcode scanner evidence to `POST /api/dispense-results` by reusing the server-side verify-barcode logic.
- files inspected: agmsg Claude approval/commit coordination, Codex read-only medical-safety/API-contract review results, `git status --short --untracked-files=all`, `src/app/api/dispense-tasks/[id]/verify-barcode/route.ts`, `src/app/api/dispense-tasks/[id]/verify-barcode/route.test.ts`, `src/app/api/dispense-results/route.ts`, `src/app/api/dispense-results/route.test.ts`, `src/lib/pharmacy/barcode.ts`, `src/app/api/__tests__/protected-post-routes.test.ts`, `CODEX_GOAL_PROGRESS.md`, and this Ralph state file.
- files changed: `src/lib/dispensing/dispense-barcode-verification.ts`, `src/app/api/dispense-tasks/[id]/verify-barcode/route.ts`, `src/app/api/dispense-results/route.ts`, `src/app/api/dispense-results/route.test.ts`, `CODEX_GOAL_PROGRESS.md`, and this Ralph state entry. Existing dirty `.codex/hooks.json`, `docs/dispensing-workbench-replacement-plan.md`, and `.agent-loop/plans/SYSTEM_UI_AUDIT_ROADMAP.md` were preserved.
- bugs found: barcode verification was route-local and could not be included in the irreversible dispense-result save. A UI scan could therefore be checked separately without server-side evidence being recorded in the safety audit for the actual save. Codex medical-safety review then found two blocking false-positive risks in the extracted helper: `{ yj_code: null }` could match a missing line drug code, and the old DrugMaster-missing GTIN prefix fallback could mark unregistered barcode evidence as a signoff match. API-contract review also found that barcode-bearing stale identical replays needed an explicit safe idempotent path and expired-barcode coverage.
- security risks found: raw barcode is accepted only as transient request input and is not persisted to AuditLog. The persisted evidence is limited to line id, match boolean, GTIN, expiry date, lot-number presence, expired flag, and warning codes. Barcode mismatch/expiry returns validation error before dispense result mutations, task updates, CDS check, workflow notification, webhook, or audit write. Match semantics now require a DrugMaster JAN/GTIN lookup plus non-empty exact YJ-code equality; null-code equality and DrugMaster-missing prefix fallback are rejected. No auth, permission, org/cycle scoping, schema, migration, external send configuration, secret, or destructive operation changed.
- performance issues found: optional barcode evidence adds at most one `DrugMaster.findFirst` per submitted scanned line and only when `barcode_scan` is present. No new dependency, polling, network call, or unbounded loop.
- validation commands: `pnpm exec vitest run 'src/app/api/dispense-tasks/[id]/verify-barcode/route.test.ts' src/app/api/dispense-results/route.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; scoped ESLint for touched files; scoped Prettier check for touched files; `git diff --check --` touched files; `pnpm typecheck`; `pnpm typecheck:no-unused`; `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/__tests__/protected-post-routes.test.ts -t "dispense-results POST" --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`.
- validation results: focused verify-barcode + dispense-results Vitest passed `2` files / `42` tests after adding null-master/null-line, no-master-prefix-fallback rejection, expired-barcode rejection, and barcode idempotent replay tests. Protected POST matrix for `dispense-results POST` passed `3` tests / `97` skipped. Scoped ESLint, Prettier check, diff whitespace check, full `pnpm typecheck`, and full `pnpm typecheck:no-unused` passed.
- remaining work: send Claude an updated `PATCH_REVIEW_REQUEST` because the first approval predates the safety tightening; address findings before commit. FE scanner UI/wiring remains Claude-owned.
- next action: run ledger format/diff check, send Claude review request, then commit after approval.

- bugs found: `PharmacyOperatingHours` existed as a real frontend/API-backed operating-hours settings model but was missing from the data-explorer coverage catalog, causing the catalog sync test to fail with `uncategorized = ['PharmacyOperatingHours']`. `PATCH /api/notifications` returned success/validation/error responses directly from `withAuthContext` without the no-store/fixed-error exported wrapper already used by `GET`, so PATCH responses could miss sensitive no-store headers and unexpected update failures lacked the route-local sanitized envelope.
- security risks found: reduced PHI/privacy cache risk by wrapping notification PATCH success, validation, auth, and unexpected error responses in `withSensitiveNoStore`, and by returning fixed `INTERNAL_ERROR` bodies after `unstable_rethrow` on unexpected failures. Privacy reviewer reported no findings and confirmed the route preserves auth behavior and does not leak raw notification/patient details in 500 bodies.
- performance issues found: no runtime performance issue introduced. The notification route query/update shape is unchanged; the added wrapper only applies response headers/fixed error handling. Catalog classification is a static list update.

Under same patient/plan, a refetch or NG classification change while the reject dialog is open can submit a terminal set-audit reject for a different reason or batch/version than the operator confirmed.
- security risks found: the remaining S1 is a patient-safety/audit-integrity risk, not an auth bypass. It weakens the irreversible confirmation boundary for set-audit reject. Additional UX safety gap: primary dialogs identify the patient by name only and reject dialog is generic, which is weak for same-name patients or adjacent set cells.
- performance issues found: none in the S0 review. The patch adds UI state checks and tests only.

- bugs found: Claude approved QR/JAHIS rev2 and packaging/facility-batch split, but correctly revised the drug-code identity C verdict to `CHANGES_REQUESTED` because `collectDuplicatePrescriptionLines` leaked the internal `code:` namespaced key into the public API `details.duplicates[].key`, breaking `prescription-intakes/route.test.ts`. Additional raw `drug_code || drug_name` identity keys remained in date continuity and prescription intake previous-diff UI, and usage-mismatch could resolve name-only QR draft medications to `DrugMaster` by `drug_name`, causing unresolved names to affect formulary/unused-stock calculations.
- security risks found: reduced medication identity and patient-safety risk by keeping namespaced `code:` / `name:` identity internal while restoring public duplicate keys to bare `drug_code` / `drug_name`; by preventing unresolved name text from matching resolved drug codes in date continuity and prescription intake diff previews; and by keeping name-only usage-mismatch QR rows unmatched instead of promoting them to DrugMaster/formulary evidence. No auth, permission, PHI logging, external send, migration application, or destructive DB behavior changed.
- performance issues found: no meaningful runtime regression. The usage-mismatch DrugMaster lookup is narrower now (`yj_code in usedCodes` only) and skips the previous name-based OR lookup. Map sizes and loop bounds are unchanged.
- validation commands: Wave1 batch1 `git show --check 51bfb537` plus `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run 'src/app/(dashboard)/patients/[id]/patient-documents-panel.test.tsx' 'src/app/(dashboard)/patients/[id]/patient-mcs-link-card.test.tsx' 'src/app/(dashboard)/patients/[id]/patient-communications-panel.test.tsx' --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; Wave1 scoped ESLint/Prettier; C rev2 `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/lib/prescription/intake-validation.test.ts src/app/api/prescription-intakes/route.test.ts 'src/app/api/qr-scan-drafts/[id]/confirm/route.test.ts' src/app/api/prescription-intakes/facility-batch/route.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; follow-up `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/app/api/pharmacy-drug-stocks/usage-mismatch/route.test.ts src/lib/dispensing/__tests__/date-continuity.test.ts 'src/app/(dashboard)/prescriptions/new/prescription-intake-form.contract.test.ts' 'src/app/(dashboard)/prescriptions/new/prescription-period-review.shared.test.ts' --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; scoped ESLint; scoped Prettier check; `git diff --check --` touched follow-up files; `pnpm typecheck`; `pnpm typecheck:no-unused`; agmsg ACK/review sends.

- bugs found: `POST /api/medication-profiles` returned patient medication profile PHI but was still exported through generic `withAuthContext`, so success/validation/not-found/auth/error responses were not all regression-locked to sensitive no-store. A privacy review also found that the first outer-wrapper attempt still allowed unexpected handler errors to be caught by the generic `withAuthContext` catch, which logs the raw `err` object before returning `internalError()`.
- security risks found: reduced PHI cache and raw-error log leakage risk by converting POST to route-local `requireAuthContext` + `runWithRequestAuthContext`, wrapping all POST responses in `withSensitiveNoStore`, and logging unexpected POST failures with only allowlisted metadata (`event`, `route`, `method`, `status`, `error_name`) while passing `undefined` as the logger error argument. Added tests proving the raw DB error string is absent from both response and logger mock calls. No auth permission, patient access check, schema, create payload, RLS transaction, DB migration, external send, or persistence behavior changed.
- performance issues found: no runtime performance issue introduced. The route keeps the same validation, patient access, and single create path; only response wrapping and catch/log behavior changed.
- validation commands: Wave1 batch2 `git show --check 8dab1167 -- 'src/app/(dashboard)/patients/[id]/card-workspace.tsx'`; `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run 'src/app/(dashboard)/patients/[id]/card-workspace.test.tsx' --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; scoped Wave1 ESLint/Prettier; `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/app/api/medication-profiles/route.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/app/api/__tests__/protected-post-routes.test.ts -t "medication-profiles POST" --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; combined targeted medication-profiles POST run; scoped ESLint; scoped Prettier; `git diff --check -- src/app/api/medication-profiles/route.ts src/app/api/medication-profiles/route.test.ts src/app/api/__tests__/protected-post-routes.test.ts`; `pnpm typecheck`; `pnpm typecheck:no-unused`.

Medical-safety reviewer reported no scoped findings; privacy reviewer findings were fixed, and privacy re-review reported no remaining scoped findings.

- security risks found: reduced set-plan detail/update PHI raw-log and cache leakage risk by converting GET/PATCH to route-local `requireAuthContext` plus `runWithRequestAuthContext`, moving GET detail reads into `withOrgContext(..., { requestContext: ctx })`, adding explicit requestContext to PATCH transaction work, wrapping exports with `withRoutePerformance` and PHI-safe try/catch, returning fixed no-store `INTERNAL_ERROR`, and logging only `event`, `route`, `method`, and allowlisted `error_name`. Reapplied assignment scoping to PATCH `updateMany` and post-update `findFirst`, and blocked stale inactive packaging method snapshots before mutation.
- performance issues found: no runtime performance regression introduced. Query/write shapes remain bounded and route metrics are preserved via explicit `withRoutePerformance`.
- validation commands: `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run 'src/app/api/set-plans/[id]/route.test.ts' --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/app/api/__tests__/protected-get-routes.test.ts -t "set-plans/\\[id\\] GET" --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/app/api/__tests__/protected-patch-delete-routes.test.ts -t "set-plans/\\[id\\] PATCH" --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; scoped `./node_modules/.bin/eslint`; scoped `./node_modules/.bin/prettier --check`; `git diff --check --` set-plans detail/protected files; `pnpm typecheck`; `pnpm typecheck:no-unused`; medical-safety and privacy reviewer inspections; agmsg inbox checks.

- bugs found: coded QR allergy entries were deduplicated by existing coded entries only. If a patient already had a legacy/manual name-only allergy with the same normalized drug name, resolving a coded QR issue could append a duplicate allergy entry and increase downstream alert/audit noise.

- files inspected: agmsg inbox/send for `phos/codex2`, `git status --short --untracked-files=all`, `docs/ui-ux-design-guidelines.md`, `node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/01-getting-started/15-route-handlers.md`, `node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/03-api-reference/01-directives/use-client.md`, `src/app/api/care-reports/[id]/send/route.ts`, `src/app/api/care-reports/[id]/send/route.test.ts`, `src/server/services/partner-visit-report-drafts.ts`, `src/server/services/partner-visit-report-drafts.test.ts`, `src/lib/db/rls.ts`, relevant partner-visit submit/review/save routes, Prisma care-report/partner-visit schemas, and reviewer outputs.
- files changed: `src/app/api/care-reports/[id]/send/route.ts`, `src/app/api/care-reports/[id]/send/route.test.ts`, this Ralph state file, and `CODEX_GOAL_PROGRESS.md`.
- bugs found: reports generated from `PartnerVisitRecord` checked the initial draft only by report status/timestamp and could still be sent after the source partner visit record was updated, returned, missing, or replaced. Revision-only provenance is unsafe because partner visit record `revision_no` is not a monotonic freshness guarantee. Initial-only freshness checks also left a race where delivery/audit/sent/event side effects could occur after the source changed.
- security risks found: reduced stale cross-organization-care communication risk by failing closed before delivery side effects, before external email send, and before non-email sent finalization. New blocked audit entries are PHI-minimized to delivery ID, report type, channel, failure reason, and source-scope booleans; they do not include patient names or recipient contact details.
- performance issues found: added bounded partner visit source reads only for partner-visit-sourced reports. No new dependency, migration, or unbounded query; the per-recipient rechecks are intentional side-effect gates.
- validation commands: `pnpm exec prettier --write 'src/app/api/care-reports/[id]/send/route.ts' 'src/app/api/care-reports/[id]/send/route.test.ts'`; `pnpm exec vitest run src/server/services/partner-visit-report-drafts.test.ts 'src/app/api/care-reports/[id]/send/route.test.ts' --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `pnpm exec eslint --max-warnings=0 'src/app/api/care-reports/[id]/send/route.ts' 'src/app/api/care-reports/[id]/send/route.test.ts'`; `pnpm exec prettier --check 'src/app/api/care-reports/[id]/send/route.ts' 'src/app/api/care-reports/[id]/send/route.test.ts'`; scoped `git diff --check`; `pnpm typecheck`; `pnpm typecheck:no-unused`; subagent reviews by `privacy_compliance_reviewer` and `medical_safety_reviewer`.
- validation results: Prettier write/check passed. Focused partner-visit draft + care-report send tests passed `2` files / `56` tests. Scoped ESLint and scoped diff-check passed. Full `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm typecheck:no-unused`, `pnpm lint`, and `pnpm format:check` passed after codex completed the unrelated prescription-history narrowing fix-forward. Privacy reviewer approved the first version. Medical safety reviewer found and rechecked fixes for revision-only provenance, transaction-race rechecks, email pre-send stale cleanup, and non-email rollback-safe cleanup; final result `APPROVED`. Claude returned `PATCH_REVIEW_RESULT: APPROVED` with one non-blocking partial-delivery observation for mid-loop multi-recipient source changes.
- remaining work: wait briefly for codex review result if it arrives, then commit only care-report send route/test plus ledgers while preserving codex-owned prescription-history/drug-master dirty paths.
- next action: stage only the four owned paths, commit the approved care-report freshness slice, send agmsg FYI, and re-check dirty tree.

### 20260630-1150 JST

- current task: extend the DrugPackage GTIN/JAN foundation into QR OTC promotion so JAN-linked OTC MedicationProfile creation no longer relies first on `DrugMaster.jan_code`.
- files inspected: `git status --short --untracked-files=all`, memory hints for master-management actual-data work, `src/server/services/qr-otc-promotion.ts`, `src/server/services/qr-otc-promotion.test.ts`, prior DrugPackage barcode verification helper/tests, `CODEX_GOAL_PROGRESS.md`, and this Ralph state file.
- files changed: `src/server/services/qr-otc-promotion.ts`, `src/server/services/qr-otc-promotion.test.ts`, `CODEX_GOAL_PROGRESS.md`, and this Ralph state file. Unrelated dirty visit-schedule/proposal files and prior master-management UI/backfill/schema WIP were preserved.
- bugs found: QR OTC promotion still resolved JAN directly through `DrugMaster.jan_code`, even after package-level `DrugPackage` identity was introduced. That left OTC MedicationProfile master-linking on the legacy single-code field and gave no fail-closed behavior for package-level ambiguity.
- security risks found: reduced medication data-integrity and patient-safety risk by resolving OTC JAN through active `DrugPackage` first. If JAN maps to multiple package DrugMasters, the promotion no longer falls back to legacy `DrugMaster.jan_code`; it creates only a name-based OTC profile with `drug_master_id: null` and duplicate checks limited to unresolved name identity. No auth/permission rule, PHI projection/logging, schema migration application, live DB data operation, external send, push, deploy, or destructive operation was performed.
- performance issues found: JAN-bearing OTC promotion adds one indexed exact-match package lookup. If a package row resolves uniquely, the legacy DrugMaster lookup is skipped. No dependency, external request, broad scan, or N+1 class was added.
- validation commands: focused Vitest on `src/server/services/qr-otc-promotion.test.ts`; related focused Vitest on QR OTC, barcode verification, dispense barcode route, dispense results, Data Explorer catalog, and Data Explorer service tests; Prettier write on QR OTC service/test; `pnpm typecheck`; `pnpm typecheck:no-unused`; `pnpm lint`; `pnpm format:check`; `git diff --check`.
- validation results: QR OTC focused test passed `1` file / `11` tests. Related DrugPackage/JAN focused suite passed `6` files / `90` tests. Prettier passed. `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm typecheck:no-unused`, `pnpm lint`, `pnpm format:check`, and `git diff --check` passed on the current dirty tree.
- remaining work: the full master-management objective remains open. Continue with DrugPackage import/backfill from official GS1/JAN/HOT sources, downstream `drug_name` keying audits, and broad DrugMaster import source-hash/publication metadata/admin confirmation hardening.
- next action: continue the next master-management roadmap slice after re-checking dirty ownership.

If package lookup resolves to a different YJ or to multiple DrugMaster YJ codes, verification now fails closed and does not fall back to legacy `DrugMaster.jan_code`. No auth/permission rule, PHI projection/logging, live DB migration application, external send, push, deploy, or destructive operation was performed.
- performance issues found: barcode verification adds one indexed exact-match `DrugPackage.findMany` lookup over GTIN/JAN candidates before the existing legacy DrugMaster lookup. If package rows are found, the legacy lookup is skipped. No dependency, unbounded scan, external request, or N+1 class was added.
- validation commands: `pnpm db:generate`; `pnpm exec prisma format --schema=prisma/schema/`; `pnpm exec prisma validate --schema=prisma/schema/`; focused Vitest for `src/lib/dispensing/dispense-barcode-verification.test.ts`, `src/app/api/dispense-tasks/[id]/verify-barcode/route.test.ts`, `src/app/api/dispense-results/route.test.ts`, `src/lib/admin/data-explorer-catalog.test.ts`, and `src/server/services/data-explorer.test.ts`; scoped Prettier check on owned TS/MD/ledger files; `pnpm typecheck`; `pnpm typecheck:no-unused`; `pnpm lint`; `pnpm format:check`; `git diff --check`.
- validation results: Prisma generate, Prisma format, and Prisma validate passed. Focused Vitest passed `5` files / `79` tests after preserving the legacy JAN fallback query order. Scoped Prettier check passed. `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm typecheck:no-unused`, `pnpm lint`, `pnpm format:check`, and `git diff --check` passed on the current dirty tree. An initial attempt to run Prettier directly on `.prisma`/`.sql` files failed because Prettier has no parser for those files; Prisma format/validate were used for schema validation instead.

Next useful slices are DrugPackage import/backfill from official GS1/JAN/HOT sources with source hash/publication metadata, downstream `drug_name` keying audits, and admin confirmation/dry-run hardening for broad DrugMaster updates.

### 20260630-1138 JST

- current task: harden schedule management writes so manual visit schedule create/PATCH reject active pharmacist or vehicle time-window double booking and recheck vehicle stop capacity inside write transactions.
- files inspected: `git status --short --untracked-files=all`, agmsg inbox/team/send for `phos/codex2`, local Next.js route-handler and `unstable_rethrow` docs, gbrain recall for schedule overlap, `src/server/services/visit-schedule-service.ts`, `src/app/api/visit-schedules/route.ts`, `src/app/api/visit-schedules/[id]/route.ts`, `src/lib/schedules/visit-schedule-conflicts.ts`, `src/lib/validations/visit-schedule.ts`, `src/lib/constants/visit.ts`, `prisma/schema/visit.prisma`, focused schedule route tests, and read-only code_mapper/concurrency/db/medical/verifier subagent findings.
- files changed: `src/server/services/visit-schedule-service.ts`, `src/app/api/visit-schedules/[id]/route.ts`, `src/app/api/visit-schedules/route.test.ts`, `src/app/api/visit-schedules/[id]/route.test.ts`, `CODEX_GOAL_PROGRESS.md`, and this Ralph state file. Unrelated prescription/drug-master/communication/care-report dirty paths were preserved.
- bugs found: manual schedule creation and PATCH enforced duplicate case/date/type and route_order conflicts but did not reject same-day active time-window overlap for the same pharmacist or selected vehicle at save time. Vehicle `max_stops` was checked before create/PATCH but not rechecked inside the write transaction, leaving a race. The first PATCH implementation also could have blocked active-to-cancelled cleanup of an already over-capacity vehicle day; medical-safety review caught this and the final condition skips vehicle capacity validation when the projected schedule no longer consumes active capacity.
- security risks found: reduced patient-safety and operational integrity risk from double-booked pharmacists, double-booked vehicles, and race-created vehicle capacity overflow. No auth/authorization rule, RLS policy, migration, live DB data operation, external send, secret handling, push, deploy, or destructive operation changed.
- performance issues found: added only bounded `findFirst` overlap checks and bounded vehicle resource/count rechecks on write paths that change active occupancy. No new dependency, unbounded loop, repeated render work, external request, or broad query fanout was added.
- validation commands: `pnpm exec prettier --write` on the four owned schedule files; `pnpm exec vitest run src/app/api/visit-schedules/route.test.ts 'src/app/api/visit-schedules/[id]/route.test.ts' --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`; `pnpm typecheck`; `pnpm test:schedule-time:tz`; `pnpm format:check`; `pnpm lint`; scoped `git diff --check` on the four owned schedule files.
- validation results: Prettier passed. Focused schedule route Vitest passed `2` files / `100` tests. `pnpm typecheck` passed. `pnpm test:schedule-time:tz` passed `30` files / `499` tests. `pnpm format:check`, `pnpm lint`, and scoped diff-check passed. codex2 returned `APPROVED`. verifier subagent confirmed focused Vitest/scoped ESLint/diff-check/typecheck passed. medical_safety_reviewer found the active-to-inactive over-capacity blocking risk; it was fixed and covered by a regression test.
- remaining work: exact-path stage only the four schedule files plus owned ledger hunks if safely separable, commit the validated schedule conflict/capacity guard slice, send agmsg FYI to codex2, then continue remaining schedule/proposal conflict candidates.
- next action: exact-path stage and commit `Harden visit schedule conflict guards`.

Blank `drug_master_id` is normalized to unspecified; unknown ids return a no-store validation error before profile creation. No auth/permission rule, schema migration, live DB operation, external send, push, deploy, secret handling, or destructive operation was performed.
- performance issues found: manual profile creation adds at most one exact `DrugMaster.findFirst` by id inside the existing RLS transaction only when `drug_master_id` is supplied. Name-only/manual profile creation keeps its previous query shape. No dependency, broad scan, external request, or N+1 class was added.

Reduced browser/proxy cache leakage and raw-error/PHI leakage risk for communication event creation responses. Tests seed patient-like names, care-manager labels, subject/content, and raw thrown details into create failures and assert fixed no-store 500 bodies do not expose those values. Existing GET behavior, assignment policy, attachment validation, contact-profile side effect, authorization rules, migrations, external sends, and live data behavior were unchanged.

Applying a selected plan by rewriting every route_order would also collide with the reorder API's confirmed-visit protection. Verifier found that the first UI source string was not accepted by the reorder API schema, which would have caused real apply requests to fail with 400 despite mocked UI tests passing.
- security risks found: reduced patient-safety/audit risk by making plan2 explicitly selectable, showing patient-confirmation impact from the selected scenario, requiring typed `再確認済み` before applying a scenario that releases one confirmed visit, passing schema-accepted `confirmation_context.source = emergency_route_interruption` into the reorder API, audit-testing that context, and excluding confirmed visits from the route_order update payload. No auth/RLS policy, PHI export, external send, migration, live DB state, secret handling, push/deploy, or destructive operation was changed.
- performance issues found: added bounded in-memory impact classification and sparse route_order assignment for the currently loaded day. No DB query, dependency, network call, broad scan, unbounded loop, external request, or heavy render path was added.
- validation commands: `pnpm exec prettier --write` on the five owned emergency-route/reorder files; focused `pnpm exec vitest run 'src/app/(dashboard)/schedules/emergency-route/emergency-route-content.test.tsx' 'src/app/api/visit-schedules/reorder/route.test.ts'`; scoped ESLint on the five owned files; scoped Prettier check on the five owned files; related `pnpm exec vitest run 'src/app/(dashboard)/schedules/schedule-day-route-order-apply.test.ts' 'src/app/(dashboard)/schedules/proposals/schedule-weekly-optimizer.test.tsx'`; `pnpm test:schedule-time:tz`; `pnpm typecheck`; `pnpm typecheck:no-unused`; `pnpm lint`; `pnpm format:check`.
- validation results: Prettier passed. Focused emergency-route/reorder suite passed `2` files / `33` tests. Related route-order/weekly-optimizer suite passed `2` files / `10` tests. `pnpm test:schedule-time:tz` passed `30` files / `518` tests. Scoped ESLint, scoped Prettier check, scoped diff-check, `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm typecheck:no-unused`, and `pnpm lint` passed. Full `pnpm format:check` is blocked by unrelated dirty `src/app/(dashboard)/patients/[id]/share/external-share-content.tsx` and `src/app/(dashboard)/patients/[id]/share/patient-share.helpers.ts`; the five owned files pass scoped Prettier check.
- remaining work: exact-path stage only the five owned emergency-route/reorder files because ledgers and many unrelated worktree paths contain mixed dirty hunks. Larger schedule-management gaps remain, including route-compare real-engine integration and broader end-to-end UI verification.
- next action: wait for verifier re-review, commit the five owned files only, notify codex2 with commit/validation summary, then continue the next clean schedule-management slice.

- files inspected: `src/server/services/visit-route-engine.ts`, `src/server/services/visit-route-engine.test.ts`, `src/server/services/visit-route-engine.locked.test.ts`, `src/app/api/visit-routes/route.ts`, `src/app/api/visit-routes/route.test.ts`, `src/types/visit-route.ts`, `package.json`, and current `git status --short --untracked-files=all`.

- bugs found: heuristic route totals accumulated only origin-to-stops travel and omitted the final stop-to-origin leg, while Google route totals are round-trip because the request destination is the origin. Vehicle duration checks that use `totalDurationSeconds` could therefore undercount heuristic routes.
- security risks found: reduced false-ok vehicle route-duration risk for visit-route planning and route comparison. No auth/RLS policy, permission, PHI export, migration, live DB operation, external send, secret handling, push/deploy, or destructive operation changed.
- performance issues found: no new DB query, network call, dependency, background job, broad scan, or unbounded loop was added. The heuristic fix reuses the already-built route matrix and reads one additional matrix cell.

- validation results: focused route suite passed `3` files / `34` tests. Schedule regression suite passed `30` files / `522` tests.
